data.text: I found the existing single-worker harness guidance in `CLAUDE.md`, but not a parallel-drain section yet. Next I’m reading the exact surrounding text and checking the tracker for the sister bead IDs so the cross-references are concrete rather than guessed.

data.output:
# Reliability Principles

See this document for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.

These principles describe how the execution machinery should behave when a layer rejects a candidate, encounters transient failure, or needs to surface degraded state to operators.

## P1: Fail-Open At Every Machinery Layer

When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event. It does not wedge the pipeline.

The auto-routing fix (`workers.go:803`, commit `3b4f5d58`) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

## P2: Single Responsibility Per Layer

Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (`fizeau` owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (`picker` owns); etc.

Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

## P3: Observable Degradation

Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

## P4: Bounded Blast Radius

A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. The stay-alive fix at commit `41cb762e` established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend it to all layers.

## P5: Operator-Visible State

Worker reports current state (`idle`, `claiming`, `executing`, `reviewing`, `blocked-on-X`) at all times. No "8 hours running, 0 attempts" mystery state.

ADR-022 rev 5 `§Probe + freshness state model` defines the worker side; the UI workers panel surfaces it.

## P6: Auto-Retry Only For Transient Classes

Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race -> no cooldown, return to ready.

Existing code: `shouldSuppressNoProgress` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1545` already respects `Disrupted` (commit `47d8054e`).

## P7: Bead = Prompt

A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked.

If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

## P8: DDx-Owned Resources Must Be Reclaimed

Every DDx-created temporary execution resource has an owner, liveness signal,
retention policy, and cleanup path. That scope includes leaked DDx test/e2e
scratch roots, execution worktrees, generated test binaries, and run-state or
liveness files. `ddx try` cleans up one attempt inline; `ddx work` cleans up
between attempts and on graceful shutdown; long-running workers also run
conservative background cleanup with a lock and jitter.

Host-level resource exhaustion is not a bead failure and not model
no-progress. If bytes, inodes, writability, or git worktree registration are
unhealthy after one cleanup pass, the work loop stops visibly and claims no more
beads. Continuing to scan the queue under ENOSPC expands the blast radius and
violates P4.

Concrete policy values — recognized DDx-owned scratch prefixes, minimum mtime
age for metadata-less directories (6 hours), soft cleanup trigger (512 MiB free
bytes / 8192 free inodes), and hard stop floor (64 MiB free bytes / 1024 free
inodes) — are defined in FEAT-010 `### Execution Resource Cleanup` and mirrored
in SD-025 `### Roots and ownership` and `### Resource preflight and loop-fatal
handling`. Implementation beads must reference those sections rather than
guessing at values. Test/e2e scratch roots under DDx prefixes (`ddx-test-`,
`ddx-e2e-`) are explicitly in scope for cleanup; treating them as out of scope
is a correctness bug under this principle.

## P9: Network-Free Drain

The single-node drain loop must never block on network I/O. `Land()` performs
only a local worktree-merge into the local target branch under a brief local
lock. `git fetch` and `git push` are never called from `Land()` or from the
`ddx work` queue loop.

This invariant prevents the 30-120 s lock holds that caused ddx-08e90869 (work
landed only on a preserved iteration ref due to tracker lock contention) and
ddx-15a51f8c (push-race remediation). Network operations are operator-driven
(see FEAT-023 `ddx sync`) or future background-deferred, never coupled to the
drain.

Governing requirement: FEAT-010 §"Network-free drain boundary" (functional
requirement 16) and §"Network isolation" (non-functional). Implementation
target: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` `Land()` and
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` drain loop — neither must call
`git fetch` or `git push`.

- `test-timing-summary.md`: Markdown table of the top 20 slowest tests (generated by `cli/tools/test-timing/`)

Use these to investigate slow-test regressions — download from the Actions run page after a push or PR.

### Test fixtures

Tests and demos that need a clean ddx-initialized git repo (without polluting this project) should use the shared fixture helper rather than rolling their own `t.TempDir()` setup. Call `testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile)` (under `cli/internal/testutils/`) which wraps `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` and auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup`. Profiles: `minimal`, `standard`, `multi-project`, `federated`. See `scripts/build-fixture-repo.md` for layout details.

Pre-commit checks include:
- Secrets detection
- Binary file prevention
- Debug statement detection
- Merge conflict detection
- DDx configuration validation
- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing

### Pre-push / CI / Local Verification Split

Pre-push hooks run cheap safety checks only (build, lint, secrets, git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug) and return fast operator feedback. The full test suite (`go test -race -cover ./...`) is **not** run on pre-push — CI on origin runs the full suite for every push to the remote, making a local duplicate redundant. Operators who want to verify before pushing can run `make test` (verbose, no race detector) or `make test-full` (mirrors CI exactly: race detector + coverage) from the `cli/` directory.

## CLI Command Overview

The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:

**Core Commands:**
- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status

**Bead Tracker:**
- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br
- `ddx bead migrate` - Externalize closed-bead events to `.ddx/attachments/` and move eligible closed beads into `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`. Idempotent; safe to re-run.

**Queue Work:**
- `ddx work` - Drain the bead execution queue (alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`; all flags pass through)

**Agent Service:**
- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
- `ddx agent execute-loop` - Drain bead queue (prefer `ddx work`)
- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
- `ddx agent log` - Session history

**Resource Commands:**
- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers

**Embedded Utilities:**
- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required

## Persona System

DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:

- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`

Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.

## When filing beads

Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.

See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.

## Execution Evidence Convention

`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — it is listed in `.gitignore` and is NOT committed to git. Files written there by agents (prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) exist only on the local machine. The durable audit trail is the events stream in `.ddx/attachments/`, not the execution dirs.

When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository working directory** — such paths are invisible to the post-execution reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific tracked in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` (which will be on-disk but untracked).

## When filing beads

A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.

In short:

- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.
- **Labels**: `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention) when applicable.

ID:       ddx-1f774edf
Title:    agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)
Type:     bug
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, area:lock, kind:fix, prevention
Owner:    erik
Deps:     ddx-3d5197d8
Desc:     PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go Land() invokes no git fetch and no git push; LandingGitOps interface no longer declares FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, or FetchOriginAncestryCheck methods.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

2. Prompt assembly uses the helper at every artifact-body interpolation site in scope.
3. `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` proves the delimiter contract and payload preservation.
4. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/agent/...` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-05T11:37:14.708641486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0e5c3005-20260505T113434-3317d134-7fb75345","depends_on_id":"ddx-d6730314","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-06T03:30:30Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Prompt-injection boundary markers are not yet explicit around artifact bodies that later flow into prompt assembly. The current server-side artifact readers in `cli/internal/server/state_executions.go:205-217` clamp text for transport, but they do not wrap the body in an explicit untrusted-data envelope for downstream prompt renderers. The review prompt path in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1561-1630` only uses XML tags for the bead envelope; it does not mark artifact content with a dedicated boundary contract.

ROOT CAUSE
There is no shared delimiter helper for untrusted artifact bodies. The current prompt builders rely on ad hoc XML wrappers and transport clamping, which are not the same thing as a prompt-injection boundary that clearly distinguishes trusted instructions from untrusted artifact text.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small helper (likely in `cli/internal/evidence` or the prompt-rendering package that owns the assembler) that wraps artifact bodies in a canonical untrusted-data delimiter pair.
- Update the prompt-rendering call sites that inline artifact bodies to use the wrapper before interpolation.
- Add a regression test named `TestArtifactBodyDelimitedAsUntrusted` that asserts the delimiter pair appears around the artifact payload and that the raw body is still preserved inside the wrapper.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not alter the transport-level clamp behavior or its `truncated` metadata.
- Do not change review verdict parsing or the FEAT-022 telemetry schema in this slice.

The guidance change is risky in two directions: it must stop broad interactive sessions from opportunistically editing ready beads, and it must not suppress non-interactive ddx work execution. The project needs deterministic regression coverage for both sides.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_guardrails_test.go contains load-bearing prompt invariant tests, but it does not assert a DDX mode boundary. Existing work-loop tests exercise no-ready-work and execution behavior, but they do not prove the new interactive queue-steward guidance is read-only or that ddx work --once still executes a ready bead after the guidance lands. The combined interactive-steward skill bead owns the fixture-backed route table and reference guidance that separate broad interactive prompts from worker prompts.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a focused regression layer that tests representative prompt routes and the worker carve-out. Use deterministic fixtures/static assertions rather than requiring an LLM call: broad interactive prompts should produce queue-steward guidance with no code-edit instruction, while ddx work/try worker prompts should produce bead_execution guidance and remain implementation-capable.

NON-SCOPE
- Benchmarking model quality.
- Running external harnesses or networked agents in unit tests.
- Replacing existing execute-bead integration tests.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-09d7d787. 7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4386b853238b444647603a77e58da0e744369010","created_at":"2026-05-09T20:58:19.197933907Z","description":"PROBLEM
  When parallel-worker drain mode is enabled (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently), bead.Store.WithLock and the agent-layer
  tracker locks become a contention surface. Today there is no
  observability into lock wait time, lock-hold duration, or contention
  rate — failures manifest as 30s MaxElapsed exhaustion and
  resource_exhausted outcomes, with no telemetry to debug.

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified this as a
  prerequisite for safely scaling up to N=2..4 parallel workers: without
  per-lock metrics, we cannot tell whether a 10s WithLock wait
  (lock.go:18-27 default) is approaching its budget under load, or
  whether 30s tracker-lock MaxElapsed (tracker_lock.go:51) is being
  exhausted by a long-tail of concurrent acquisitions vs occasional
  hot-path blocking.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27 WithLock has no instrumentation; it
    spins at 50ms intervals and waits up to 10s by default. Wait time is
    not measured or emitted.
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183 withTrackerLock /
    withMainGitLock acquire/release path has no instrumentation.
  - There is no operator-facing dashboard, log, or metric for lock
    contention.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add lightweight metrics around three lock surfaces:

  1. bead.Store.WithLock (cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27):
     - Record lock-wait duration (time from WithLock entry to lock
       acquisition).
     - Record lock-hold duration (time the callback held the lock).
     - Emit via the existing agentmetrics package (or a new bead-metrics
       channel) keyed by call site.

  2. withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock (cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183):
     - Same wait/hold instrumentation.
     - Additional: record retry-attempt count when DefaultLockRetryPolicy
       is consumed (so we see "needed 3 retries" vs "got it on first
       attempt").

  3. Exit code / stop reason aggregation:
     - When a worker times out at MaxElapsed, capture the lock identity
       and the holder's claimant-PID at point of exhaustion. Today the
       error message is generic.

  Surface format:
     - Existing agentmetrics output (likely .ddx/agent-metrics or
       similar; check cli/internal/agentmetrics for existing API).
     - Or stdout JSON line per lock acquire/release if no agentmetrics
       sink exists.

  Add tests:
     - TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration: assert metric is captured for a
       contended lock.
     - TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount: assert retry count metric is
       captured under simulated contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Fixing lock contention itself (sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes the
    big contributor; this bead is observability for what's left).
  - Building a lock-contention dashboard (separate concern; just emit
    the data).
  - Reducing the 10s/30s budgets (those are correct; the metric tells
    us if they're tight).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land()) drops the big
    contention cause; this bead provides visibility into what remains.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a (chaos for stale-lock recovery) covers
    correctness; this bead covers visibility.
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain
    landing under shared-main contention" needs this metrics surface to
    write meaningful assertions.","events_attachment":"ddx-128c7f9d/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-128c7f9d","issue_type":"chore","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:feature","observability","prevention"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"7","session_id":"eb-2b4c3d8d","status":"closed","title":"agent/lock: instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time + tracker-lock acquisition metrics","updated_at":"2026-05-10T15:53:25.567407957Z"}

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified a gap in the
  operator playbook: when running multiple ddx work --once shells in
  parallel (after ddx-1f774edf lands), operators must understand that
  using the same harness across all N workers will throttle on the
  per-account rate limit (e.g., Anthropic 5-tier rate limits for the
  claude harness). Mixing harnesses across workers (claude + codex +
  openrouter, etc.) dodges per-harness ceilings and delivers true N×
  throughput. There is no operator documentation today that says this.

CURRENT STATE
  - CLAUDE.md describes the project structure and per-CLI commands but
    not the operator playbook for parallel drains.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/ contains reliability principles and alignment
    reviews but no operator-facing parallel-drain guide.
  - .agents/skills/ddx/reference/ and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/
    contain skill documentation but not an explicit
    parallel-drain-playbook section.
  - CLAUDE.md memory note feedback_models.md says "prefer claude harness
    for execute-loop; avoid agent harness for real work" — the harness
    selection guidance exists for single-worker drain but not for
    parallel.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/ if
  preferred for separation of concerns) titled "Parallel drain
  operator playbook" covering:

  1. When to use parallel drain: after ddx-1f774edf lands; not before
     (lock contention defeats the parallelism today).

  2. How to start: open N tmux panes / shells, each running a
     `ddx work --once` loop. No code change required.

  3. Harness mixing: example of running claude on one shell, codex on
     another, openrouter on a third — mixing dodges per-harness rate
     limits and avoids serializing on Anthropic quota.

  4. Cost ceiling: each ddx work invocation accepts --max-cost; budget
     across N shells.

  5. Conflict expectations: parallel landing produces some preservation
     under load (covered by the chaos sister bead); set operator
     expectation that occasional preservation is normal and the
     preserved iteration refs can be merged manually.

  6. Observability: point at the lock-metrics sister bead's output
     channels (once landed) for debugging contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a `ddx work --parallel N` flag (deliberately YAGNI per
    plan review; external shells deliver the same throughput).
  - Server-spawned-worker design (ADR-022 step 7, separate work).
  - Harness-selection algorithm (single-worker memory note already
    covers default selection).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister beads: ddx-1f774edf (must land before parallel drain is
    safe), the chaos test bead, the lock-metrics bead.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 3 candidates rejected"}","created_at":"2026-05-12T02:46:06.85555782Z","kind":"bead-quality.lint","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"warning score=0"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T02:45:01.136030592Z","id":"ddx-170bea78","issue_type":"chore","labels":["phase:6","area:docs","area:operator","kind:doc","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"9","status":"in_progress","title":"docs(operator): document harness-mixing for parallel ddx work shells","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:46:06.900361993Z"}

2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.

8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T00:44:33Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"4014696","closing_commit_sha":"dd027a1a9aaa3a56cd3f1a2bad0c15a3e8583f5a","created_at":"2026-05-09T19:44:54.281360848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-1f774edf","depends_on_id":"ddx-3d5197d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-09T19:53:25Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.","events_attachment":"ddx-1f774edf/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T00:44:33.669520731Z","id":"ddx-1f774edf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:fix","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"2","session_id":"eb-876e92e7","status":"closed","title":"agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:35:01.204479139Z"}

The drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE

  After a successful bead land, the post-land
  syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths step overwrites the live
  .ddx/beads.jsonl with the version committed in the landed worktree.
  That committed version is a snapshot from when the agent claimed
  the bead — not the authoritative current state. Any operator
  writes to beads.jsonl that happened after the claim (e.g.,
  `ddx bead update <id> --set queue-rank=N`, dep edits, priority
  bumps) are silently reverted on the next land.

  Observed: I set queue-rank on ~12 beads via `ddx bead update --set
  queue-rank=N`. Later, after the drain landed unrelated beads, all
  queue-rank values had vanished (parseQueueRank returned empty).
  The drain reverted to default sort (priority + creation date),
  ignoring my prioritization.

  This is a silent data loss. No error is surfaced. The operator's
  intended ordering simply stops working after the next land.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:384 RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    delegates to syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:429
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths runs `git checkout-index -f` for
    every file in the fromRev..HEAD diff, except those listed in
    skipPaths (today: only files dirty-before-land are skipped via
    dirtyBefore guard).
  - .ddx/beads.jsonl is committed inside the landing worktree as
    part of agent evidence (claim, heartbeat, close events). It
    therefore appears in the fromRev..HEAD diff at land time.
  - The dirty-file guard at land time does NOT save it: if the
    operator's `--set` write was already committed via
    beadAutoCommit (which the agent itself runs to checkpoint
    tracker state), the file appears "clean" at land time → not
    skipped → got overwritten with the older landed version.
  - Same bug applies to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same
    mechanism.

  No code path in cli/internal/bead/ explicitly clears queue-rank;
  the marshal round-trip preserves unknown Extra keys correctly.
  This is purely a git-overwrite issue at the agent/land seam.

PROPOSED FIX
  Treat .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl as live-state
  files managed by the bead.Store lock — never authoritative from a
  landed commit. They must be UNCONDITIONALLY skipped by
  SyncWorkTreeToHead, regardless of dirty-before-land state.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go RealLandingGitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead
    (line 384 area): change call signature to pass an unconditional
    skip list:
        return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, []string{
            ".ddx/beads.jsonl",
            ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
        })
    Or: introduce a new constant
        var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{".ddx/beads.jsonl", ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"}
    and merge with the existing dirtyBefore set inside
    syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths.
  - Document at the call site why these paths are special: they are
    governed by bead.Store WithLock; the committed worktree version
    is a stale snapshot.
  - Add the regression test described in AC.

  ADDITIONALLY, add comprehensive lifecycle preservation tests for
  queue-rank to cover the full set of transitions that operators
  expect to be safe (zero tests exist today for this).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring SyncWorkTreeToHead beyond the always-skip list.
  - Other "live state" files in .ddx/ that might have similar
    issues (executions/, attachments/) — those are append-only or
    per-attempt and don't share the snapshot problem; only the
    JSONL stores do.
  - The network-free Land() refactor (sister bead ddx-1f774edf will
    rewrite this file; this fix should land first or be merged into
    that work).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land code) will refactor
    execute_bead_land.go heavily. This bead's fix should land first
    so subsequent drain prioritization is reliable; otherwise
    ddx-1f774edf's own queue-rank may be wiped before it gets
    picked up.","events_attachment":"ddx-2257460f/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-2257460f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:bead","kind:fix","observed-failure","prevention","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"0","session_id":"eb-db57e72d","status":"closed","title":"fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead clobbers .ddx/beads.jsonl, wiping live state","updated_at":"2026-05-10T12:19:56.921284949Z"}

The production cleanup manager is too narrow for the resource failure we just had to repair by hand. ddx cleanup reported little or no reclaimable space because it only owns execution worktree directories, while DDx-generated e2e/test scratch directories and generated test binaries can consume many GiB in the same temp filesystem.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:202-212 reads entries only under the execution temp root and skips anything without ExecuteBeadWtPrefix. This excludes DDx-owned scratch prefixes created by command/e2e tests and generated binaries.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:234-240 preserves ownership mismatches unconditionally, which is correct for foreign projects but means test-owned DDx execution worktrees are never reclaimed by an operator running cleanup from the main project.
- cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-67 constructs the cleanup manager with default scope only; there is no CLI or manager option to include safe DDx scratch prefixes.
- cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:69-88 summary fields only count execution temp dirs, registered worktrees, and run-state files, so broader scratch cleanup would not be observable without new fields.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend ExecutionCleanupManager with an explicit DDx scratch cleanup scope driven by allowlisted prefixes/metadata, minimum age, and liveness/process checks.
- Include DDx-created e2e/test scratch roots and generated test binary directories in that scope without touching arbitrary temp directories.
- Keep foreign-project execution worktree preservation by default, but add a safe test-owned/expired-DDx metadata path when liveness is stale and the directory is not registered active.
- Extend cleanup summaries and CLI JSON/human output with scratch dirs removed, scratch bytes/inodes reclaimed, preserved active scratch count, and warnings.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete non-DDx temp directories or user-managed caches.
- Do not remove complete .ddx/executions, .ddx/runs, refs/ddx/iterations/*, active registered worktrees, or live liveness-marked paths.
- Do not change work-loop stop policy; ddx-fc6b42d2 wires cleanup into the claim cycle after this bead.

Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:tooling","kind:study","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-07T16:13:04.858647865Z"}

6. lefthook run pre-commit passes for tracker-only changes before implementation starts.","created_at":"2026-05-08T19:01:32.510635383Z","description":"PROBLEM
The bead lifecycle is currently controlled by a mix of status values, lifecycle labels, ad hoc events, retry metadata, and command-specific conditionals. That makes ddx work unreliable: execution-ready work, operator-required work, dependency waits, and terminal outcomes can drift apart depending on which surface is queried.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:38 defines the six upstream status values, but later sections still make labels such as needs_human and triage:needs-investigation lifecycle-driving states.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1450 filters needs-investigation and needs_human labels in readiness logic, while cli/internal/bead/store.go:1548 treats ordinary dependency waiting as Blocked().
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1961 parks intake rejections by adding a needs_human label instead of moving the bead lifecycle into a single status field.
- cli/internal/agent/try/no_changes.go:60 parses status: needs_investigation even though that value is not an upstream bead status.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:177 exposes needsHuman counts as lifecycle output, and the Svelte bead page hard-codes derived ready/incomplete status options.

PROPOSED FIX
Replace conflicting lifecycle behavior with one DDx-owned status state machine. Status is the sole lifecycle field. The accepted lifecycle values are proposed, open, in_progress, blocked, closed, and cancelled. proposed means operator decision required; open means accepted active work and may be dependency-waiting; in_progress means claimed/running; blocked means accepted work paused by an external recheckable blocker; closed means terminal satisfied and satisfies dependents; cancelled means terminal not-doing and does not satisfy dependents. Dependency waiting is derived from dependency closure, not status=blocked. needs_human and triage:needs-investigation stop being queue labels and become legacy metadata/events only where needed for migration or compatibility.

NON-SCOPE
Do not execute the refactor in this epic. Child beads own specs, pure state machine code, store/query wiring, agent outcome mapping, API/UI surfaces, skill contracts, and final conformance.

CHILD WORK
The children under this epic must keep the same invariant: lifecycle decisions call the pure state-machine contract rather than adding new one-off conditionals.","execution-eligible":false,"id":"ddx-349e8e03","issue_type":"epic","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","area:agent","kind:epic","lifecycle-state-machine","spec:TD-031","spec:FEAT-004","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":0,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"closed","title":"bead: replace lifecycle labels with status-owned state machine","updated_at":"2026-05-09T14:50:15.65253751Z"}

Even after a pure lifecycle state machine exists, DDx can still drift if status writers bypass it. Today commands and store helpers assign statuses directly, so invalid or semantically wrong transitions can keep entering the tracker from CLI, GraphQL, REST/MCP, claim/unclaim, close/reopen, or operator-prompt paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:535-562 exposes Store.Update, where callers can mutate b.Status directly before generic validation.
- cli/cmd/bead.go:525-526 assigns `b.Status = v` for `ddx bead update --status`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:580-612 implements ClaimWithOptions with direct status checks and assignment.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:730-736 makes Unclaim set in_progress back to open directly.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1024-1027 and cli/internal/bead/store.go:1192-1209 close/reopen by assigning statuses directly.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go:228-256 currently resolves human-review state by mutating labels/closing rather than lifecycle transitions.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a status-transition write API, for example Store.TransitionLifecycle or Store.SetLifecycleStatus, that delegates to the pure lifecycle transition function and records transition reason/evidence. Route every status-writing path through it: update --status, claim, unclaim, close, close-with-evidence, reopen, operator prompt approve/cancel, GraphQL/REST/MCP update, migration application, and agent parking paths. Leave Store.Update for non-lifecycle fields but prevent arbitrary status assignment outside the transition API.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change ready/blocked query derivation in this bead. Do not add broad backcompat for legacy labels; old lifecycle labels are handled only by the one-way migrator.

9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4386b853238b444647603a77e58da0e744369010","created_at":"2026-05-09T19:52:26.846044896Z","description":"PROBLEM
  Today's Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go performs git
  fetch, push --ff-only, and push-race auto-recovery as part of the core
  drain flow. This causes 30-120s lock holds spanning network I/O, which
  in turn produced ddx-08e90869 (work landed only on a preserved iteration
  ref because tracker lock contention prevented the main merge) and
  ddx-15a51f8c (push-race remediation commit). The user's architectural
  position is: Land() should perform only the worktree-merge into local
  main; origin-sync (fetch) and push are separate concerns, operator-driven
  or background-deferred, never coupled to Land() or the drain.

  The spec corpus partially reflects this position already:
  FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares "DDx does not invoke `git push` at
  all." But FEAT-010 (the governing feature for task execution) does not
  state the network-free boundary as a requirement, and the multi-machine
  docs (API-001 §"Land And Preserve Semantics", SD-020) describe a
  push/fetch-coupled lifecycle that is correct for distributed mode but
  contradicts the single-node invariant.

  This is a doc-only bead. It lands the requirement in the right anchor
  spec, then reconciles every cross-reference so the spec corpus is
  coherent and complete. Implementation removal of push/fetch from Land()
  is a separate sister bead (ddx-1f774edf, rewritten in parallel).

ROOT CAUSE
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md ##Requirements
    section (around line 1057) enumerates functional + non-functional
    requirements but has no "Network isolation" or "Sync decoupling"
    requirement.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-git-awareness.md:134 declares the
    correct invariant ("DDx does not invoke `git push` at all") but has no
    cross-reference to FEAT-010 or to the implementation status.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md
    Layer 2/3 sections do not mention push/fetch — silent on the boundary,
    so implementers may inadvertently reintroduce network I/O.
  - docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:221-225
    §"Land And Preserve Semantics" prescribes "fetch origin before rebase"
    and "push --ff-only ... atomic ref-update is the serialization point".
    This was written for the multi-machine coordinator topology and
    contradicts the single-node invariant.
  - docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-020-multi-machine-coordinator-topology.md
    lines 51, 60, 79, 83, 117, 123, 136, 155 reference git push/fetch as
    part of the lifecycle; these are correctly scoped to multi-machine but
    need an explicit preamble distinguishing single-node from multi-machine.
  - docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-023-sync.md exists but does not
    explicitly position itself as the designated home for origin-sync
    semantics complementing FEAT-010's drain boundary.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md enumerates P1-P8
    execution-loop invariants; the network-free drain is the natural P9.

PROPOSED FIX
  All edits are doc-only:

  1. FEAT-010: under ##Requirements, add a new functional requirement:
     "Network-free drain boundary: Land() performs only the worktree-merge
     into local main, under a brief local lock. Origin-sync (git fetch +
     merging origin/main into local main) and origin-push (git push) are
     separate operations, operator-driven or background-deferred, and are
     never invoked from Land() or from the drain loop."
     Plus a non-functional requirement bullet:
     "Network isolation: single-developer/single-node drain does not block
     on remote I/O; origin sync and push are decoupled and may run in
     parallel or in the background without holding the drain or land lock."

  2. FEAT-012:134 — keep the existing line; add a cross-reference sentence
     pointing to FEAT-010's new requirement and noting the implementation
     gap is being closed by the sister implementation bead.

  3. SD-025 — under Layer 2 (bead attempt) and Layer 3 (queue drain), add a
     one-sentence cross-reference: "Per FEAT-010 network-free drain
     boundary, this layer performs no network I/O; origin-sync and push are
     out-of-band per FEAT-023."

  4. API-001:221-225 §"Land And Preserve Semantics" — add an explicit
     preamble: "These semantics describe the multi-machine coordinator
     topology (SD-020). For single-node mode (FEAT-010 default), Land() is
     local-only; the fetch+push lifecycle described below applies only when
     multi-machine coordination is active."

  5. SD-020 — at the top of the doc, add a one-paragraph preamble making
     the single-node vs multi-machine distinction explicit, with a pointer
     to FEAT-010.

  6. FEAT-023-sync.md — add a sentence positioning it as the designated
     home for origin-sync semantics, complementing FEAT-010's drain
     boundary. Defer the actual `ddx sync` helper to the future
     multi-machine work; this is just a naming/scoping clarification.

  7. reliability-principles.md — add P9 "Network-free drain": short
     principle text, file:line citations to FEAT-010 and Land(),
     justification (single-node drain must not block on network I/O).

NON-SCOPE
  - The implementation removal of push/fetch from Land() and the
    LandingGitOps interface (sister bead ddx-1f774edf, rewritten).
  - Designing or building a `ddx sync` helper (downstream of multi-node
    work; the user explicitly deferred this).
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact, separate beads).
  - Changing TD-031 (state machine is unaffected).
  - Changing ADR-022 (worker client-server is orthogonal).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister implementation bead ddx-1f774edf depends on this landing.
  - Future multi-node work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is a temporary
    scoping that explicitly says "single-node only" so future-spec authors
    can layer multi-node semantics on top.","events_attachment":"ddx-3d5197d8/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-3d5197d8","issue_type":"chore","labels":["phase:6","area:docs","area:agent","kind:doc","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"1","session_id":"eb-2255e344","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"docs(FEAT-010): declare network-free drain boundary; reconcile cross-refs across spec corpus","updated_at":"2026-05-10T15:52:35.634791556Z"}

  After sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the
  drain becomes safe for external parallelism (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently). However, parallel workers will land their
  own bead's worktree against the same local main and may produce
  overlapping changes — multi-worker conflict frequency on Land()'s
  git merge step is unmeasured. The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus
  self-review identified this as a key risk: under high parallelism,
  preservation (resource_exhausted to iteration ref) could become the
  common path rather than the exception, defeating the gain.

  This bead adds chaos coverage for the parallel-landing case and a
  sentinel that fails if the preservation rate exceeds an acceptable
  threshold under simulated load.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go cover
    bead.Store-layer concurrency only.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go covers single-worker
    semantics.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a covers stale-lock recovery for a single
    worker's local-only land. It does not cover N>1 workers landing in
    parallel.
  - No test verifies what happens when worker A lands branch-A into
    main, then worker B (whose worktree was branched from pre-A main)
    tries to land branch-B with overlapping changes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new
  file, or extend tracker_lock_test.go):

  1. TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each with its own bead/worktree
       touching disjoint files.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: all three land on local main, in some order; no
       preservation; main contains all three commits.

  2. TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each touching the same file with
       different content.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: exactly one lands; the other two are preserved to
       iteration refs; the preserved branches are intact and contain
       their bead's full work.

  3. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded:
     - Spawn N worker goroutines (N=4) over M cycles (M=10), each with
       a randomized mix of overlapping/non-overlapping changes.
     - Assert: preservation rate is < 50% (i.e., at least half of
       attempts land cleanly). This is a smoke check that parallel
       drain is viable, not a hard SLA.

  Use deterministic timing (channels or synctest), not wall-clock.
  Use testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir() per test.
  Depends on sister bead ddx-1f774edf so the lock holds are sub-second
  (otherwise the test exercises today's contention bug, not the
  parallel-landing concern).

NON-SCOPE
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps that mirror the real merge logic).
  - Coordinating workers via a "land queue" abstraction (separate
    architectural concern; this bead establishes the baseline measurement
    against which any such work would be evaluated).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (depends on it landing first).
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/lock: instrument bead.Store
    WithLock wait time" — its metrics are referenced by these tests'
    assertions if available.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a complements this for the single-worker
    stale-lock case.","events_attachment":"ddx-42741260/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T02:35:01.729089895Z","id":"ddx-42741260","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:test","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"8","session_id":"eb-f18b8501","status":"closed","title":"agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain landing under shared-main contention","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:45:00.616545921Z"}

<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt · Inspect and adapt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Use ddx bead commands for tracker changes · Preserve audit evidence · Keep prompts fit for execution and self-contained · Go implementation follow-up must use real/stubbed domain interfaces and go test plus lefthook gates</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 / ADR-023 readiness policy currently permits safe intent-preserving updates before claim; this evolve pass specifies the stronger validated replacement rewrite contract so readiness can improve prompt fitness while preserving explicit commitments.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite policy is currently ambiguous. The append-only preservation guard prevents deletion of stale or noisy wording, but appending more text can make future bead prompts longer and more confusing. DDx needs specs that define validated replacement rewrites as a first-class readiness capability, including both expansion of underspecified beads and compression of noisy/stale beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-196 defines actionable_but_rewritten but does not say replacement rewrites are allowed or how they are validated.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-144 says readiness may update beads but does not distinguish append-only amendments from validated replacement rewrites.
- docs/triage/decomposition.md:17-26 describes safe rewrites but does not require prompt fitness or audit evidence for replaced text.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:392-407 names readiness transitions but does not model successful replacement rewrite as an open-to-open readiness mutation with evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Update the governing specs to define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: replacement bead text is allowed when a deterministic validator preserves intent, acceptance criteria, non-scope, durable evidence, and unresolved product choices; original text is retained in audit evidence, not duplicated into the prompt body. Replacement can expand an underspecified bead or compress a noisy one when prompt fitness requires it.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement code changes in this evolve pass.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not permit rewrites that choose missing product semantics or delete unresolved conflicts.

PARENT + DEPS

Unit/integration test covers: worker returns task_succeeded → pre-merge check blocks → evidence event present, iteration ref written, bead unclaimed for retry.","claimed-at":"2026-05-09T12:04:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"877271","closing_commit_sha":"0526a3b36a37e76ac133fc33bdf5b090695d89f9","created_at":"2026-05-08T19:14:19.126446969Z","description":"Context:
When `SubmitWithPreMergeChecks()` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:131-178) calls `RunPreMergeChecks()` and the outcome is Blocked, `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` writes the result under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<basesha>` and skips the merge. The bead's evidence list never receives a `pre_merge_check_failed` event with the check name, output, or rationale.

This made a real incident on 2026-05-08 invisible: bead `fizeau-bd5193ca` (in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) was redriven 4 times across ~50 minutes / ~$9 of agent cost. Each attempt's result.json reported `outcome: task_succeeded, status: success` (worker classification, written before the gate) and the orchestrator silently preserved the result. `ddx bead evidence list fizeau-bd5193ca` only showed routing + cost events; `ddx bead show` had no hint that pre-merge checks had blocked anything. Operator had no way to discover the rejection without reading orchestrator source.

Reproduction:
1. Claim any bead via `ddx work` whose worker output trips a pre-merge check.
2. Observe result.json status=success, iteration ref preserved, bead remains open, evidence list devoid of pre-merge-check info.

Proposed fix:
- Emit a structured `pre_merge_check_failed` evidence event from `PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks()` (or its caller) with at least: check_id/name, exit_code, stdout/stderr tail, attempt_id, base_rev, result_rev. Append via the same evidence path used by routing/cost events so it shows up in `ddx bead evidence list` and `ddx bead show`.
- The orchestrator's terminal status (post worker + gate) should also be reflected back into result.json's status field — currently it stays `success` regardless of the gate outcome, which is misleading. Either rewrite result.json with the orchestrator's verdict, or add a separate `orchestrator_status` field.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"from_status=closed

Agents should use the prose workflow without being reminded. The human-writing-support skill needs examples and workflow steps that match the Vale-backed `ddx doc prose` command.

ROOT CAUSE
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-06-prose-quality-integration.md:193-262 defines the skill trigger, workflow, preservation rules, examples, and evals, but the active and shipped skill copies need to be upgraded after the Vale-backed command exists.

SMART JUSTIFICATION: Skill behavior is high-judgment because it controls how agents apply prose findings. A weak skill will either ignore findings or over-edit technical docs, commands, tables, IDs, and acceptance criteria.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update active and shipped `human-writing-support` skill copies.
- Add examples for unsupported claims, AI-slop paragraphs, token-cost edits, false positives, and table/path/API preservation.
- Instruct agents to run and rerun `ddx doc prose --changed`.
- Add or update skill eval prompts if the repo supports them.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Vale rule semantics.
- Do not make prose findings blocking.
- Do not edit unrelated skills.

DEPS

Long-running DDx entrypoints can start work even when the project-local skill install is broken. In /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, post-attempt triage logged `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)` only after an execution failed. The operator had to infer that the project still had a pre-migration skill layout and needed `ddx update --force` / `ddx doctor`.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/cmd/work.go:106` delegates directly to `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl` without a lightweight runtime preflight. `cli/cmd/try.go:102-120` resolves execution flags and proceeds toward store/runtime setup without checking project-local skill prerequisites. `cli/cmd/server.go:98-146` starts the server without surfacing degraded project-local skill layout. `cli/cmd/doctor.go:281-287` already detects legacy skill symlinks and prints `run: ddx update --force`, but that diagnostic is only available when the operator explicitly runs `ddx doctor`.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a shared lightweight project-runtime preflight helper for long-running entrypoints. It should reuse existing doctor/install helper logic where practical, but must not run the full `ddx doctor` checks.
- Wire the helper into `ddx work`, `ddx try`, compatibility `ddx agent execute-loop`, and `ddx server` before execution begins.
- For work/try/execute-loop: fail on missing required project/tracker state, warn once per process for optional lifecycle-hook degradation in WARN-ONLY mode, and include remediation.
- For server: fail on required server project state, but log degraded startup diagnostics for missing lifecycle skills or legacy skill symlink layout.
- Checked paths for `bead-lifecycle` must be `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` under the resolved project root.
- Remediation text must include `ddx update --force` first, then `ddx doctor`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add network, package-manager, route/provider, stale-doc, or Fizeau health probes to this preflight.
- Do not change bead-lifecycle hook classification semantics.
- Do not make `ddx server` refuse to start solely because `bead-lifecycle` is missing.
- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually; tracker mutation is handled by this bead record only.

The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend the backend test helpers so the package conformance suite can run under both jsonl and axon.
- Keep TestChaos_ConcurrentAppendSafety, TestChaos_AtomicStatusTransitions, TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, and TestChaos_ConcurrentCloseNotLost as explicit backend matrix coverage.
- Make the package test harness clearly fail if either backend diverges.

NON-SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:229:{"acceptance":"1. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsCompressionReplacement verifies a replacement description can remove stale/noisy old prose when explicit commitments and durable anchors are preserved.
2. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_AllowsExpansionReplacement verifies a vague one-line description can expand into a standalone executable bead when added content is grounded in durable context and does not invent product semantics.
3. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RejectsDeletedCommitment verifies non-scope, governing artifact references, named files/tests, or unresolved constraints cannot be silently removed.
4. TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite_RecordsReplacementEvidence verifies `intake-rewritten` records changed fields, rationale, before/after hashes, and preservation evidence sufficient to audit the original text without duplicating it into the replacement prompt body.
5. TestPreClaimIntakePrompt_AsksForFitForPurposeValidatedReplacement verifies the prompt tells the model to optimize prompt fitness, avoid append-only noise, and classify ambiguous cases as needs-human.
6. Existing TestIntake_ActionableButRewritten_UpdatesAfterClaim and TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteBlocksForHuman remain green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreClaimIntakeRewrite|TestIntake_.*Rewrite" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"485f957bafa6e880b7bb613769f2b3876f85feb8","created_at":"2026-05-08T00:37:29.215144286Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-a41e6fbb","depends_on_id":"ddx-5cd555c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-08T00:37:43Z"}],"description":"<context-digest>
<principles>Spec-first development · Executable specifications · Audit trail required · Context is king · Drift is debt</principles>
<concerns>go-std | security-owasp | testing</concerns>
<practices>Go changes live under cli/ · prefer focused unit tests with in-memory stubs for domain interfaces · run targeted go test plus lefthook · preserve audit evidence for automated mutations</practices>
<governing>FEAT-010 and ADR-023 now define safe rewrites as validated replacement rewrites: fit-for-purpose replacement is allowed when preservation is proven from durable anchors; original text belongs in readiness evidence, not duplicated into the bead prompt.</governing>
</context-digest>

PROBLEM
Safe rewrite implementation still enforces append-only description preservation even though the governing specs now require validated replacement rewrites. This keeps stale/noisy bead text in the implementation prompt and rejects useful replacements; it also fails the opposite case, where a one-line vague bead must expand into a standalone executable task.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:118-120 rejects any description rewrite unless the normalized old description appears as a substring of the new description.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:183-188 prompts the intake model to return rewrites but does not say replacement rewrites should optimize prompt fitness, validate against durable anchors, and preserve original text through evidence rather than prompt duplication.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:176-211 and docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md:136-166 now require validated replacement rewrites, prompt fitness, and audit evidence.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:60-80 records before/after hashes but does not preserve enough original/replacement evidence or preservation rationale for audit when the old body is replaced.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the substring-only description guard with a deterministic validated-replacement policy. The validator must preserve explicit commitments: acceptance criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies, named files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence, or replace stale evidence with current durable file:line/section anchors.
- Allow both directions of prompt-fitness improvement: expanding underspecified beads with necessary durable context, and compressing noisy/stale beads while preserving explicit commitments.
- Keep rejection behavior for invented behavior, changed scope, deleted unresolved constraints, or missing preservation evidence.
- Update the intake prompt to ask for fit-for-purpose replacement rewrites, durable preservation evidence, and explicit ambiguity instead of append-only amendments.
- Extend the `intake-rewritten` event body so original/replacement audit evidence and preservation rationale are available without embedding the old body back into the new prompt. Use hashes plus bounded excerpts or attachment pointers consistent with existing event-size conventions.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement child decomposition changes.
- Do not relax acceptance criteria preservation.
- Do not allow product-semantic choices to be made by the rewrite validator.
- Do not rename legacy `PreClaimIntake*` structs in this bead.

PARENT + DEPS
