.ddx/beads.jsonl:238:{"acceptance":"1. Protocol spec: .ddx/checks/*.yaml schema documented; Go types in cli/internal/checks/protocol.go match the spec; JSON output schema documented.
2. Loader reads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml at runtime; invalid YAML or unknown fields fail loudly with file:line.
3. Runner invokes the configured command with documented env vars; reads ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json; treats non-zero exit as status=error.
4. ddx try integration: pre_merge hook runs ALL applicable checks (filtered by applies_to) in parallel before merge-back; any status=block or status=error aborts the merge and preserves the worktree per existing iteration-preservation conventions.
5. checks_bypass annotation on the bead skips the named check and records an event with the reason; missing reason = bypass rejected.
6. ddx ac run <bead-id> standalone subcommand runs the same checks against the current worktree state and prints results; exit 1 on any block/error.
7. End-to-end test: a fixture .ddx/checks/dummy-fail.yaml with a script that always returns status=block. Run ddx try on a no-op bead; observe the merge is aborted, worktree preserved, evidence file written, event recorded.
8. End-to-end test: same fixture + checks_bypass annotation on the bead → merge proceeds, bypass event recorded.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/checks/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.
10. Evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ shows the check fixture wrote its result.json and ddx parsed it correctly.","closing_commit_sha":"21696af5753c22de0af06641243a30de3900e1c6","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:34:44.62487483Z","description":"Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-07T05:58:27.033782082Z","kind":"lifecycle_reconciled","source":"ddx bead reconcile","summary":"closed bead has stale no_changes lifecycle metadata"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"from_status=closed

.ddx/beads.jsonl:267:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:55.438930116Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bca628fa","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 lifecycle-transition methods (`TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed`) that encode HELIX-flavored state-machine policy directly in the storage layer. Callers in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, and `cli/internal/server/graphql/` hold `*Store` concretely and invoke these methods. Per TD-027's storage-vs-workflow separation, the state-machine validation belongs in a workflow helper package, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, op Operation)` plus typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes lifecycle methods that mutate status with HELIX-specific transition validation embedded. `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, and `cli/cmd/bead.go` call these methods directly. The new `Operation` pattern (added in Foundation bead `ddx-c6317784`) provides the primitive but the workflow helpers don't exist yet.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §14 helper package `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/`:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`: `SetStatus{Status}`, `SetLifecycleStatus{Status, Options}`, `SetCloseEvidence{SessionID, CommitSHA}`, `ReopenOp{Reason, Notes}`, `ParkToProposedOp{Reason}`, `AppendNotes{Notes}`.
2. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/transition.go` with helpers:
   - `Transition(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, r BeadReader, id, newStatus string) error` — validates against TD-027 §2 transition matrix; calls `l.Apply(ctx, id, SetLifecycleStatus{Status: newStatus})`.
   - `CloseWithEvidence(ctx, l, id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error`.
   - `Reopen(ctx, l, id, reason, notes string) error`.
   - `ParkToProposed(ctx, l, id, reason string) error`.
   - `AppendNotes(ctx, l, id, notes string) error`.
   - `SetStatus(ctx, l, id, status string) error` — for callers that don't need state-machine validation.
3. Migrate callers:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` — `store.Reopen(...)`, `store.CloseWithEvidence(...)`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(...)` → `lifecycle.Reopen(ctx, store, ...)` etc.
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go` — `store.TransitionLifecycle`, `store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `store.Reopen` → helper calls.
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` — lifecycle transition usages → helpers.
   - All test files that use these methods (where they assert workflow behavior) → helpers; tests that assert storage primitive behavior stay using `Apply` directly.
4. Remove `*Store.TransitionLifecycle`, `SetLifecycleStatus`, `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `AppendNotes`, `Reopen`, `ParkToProposed` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Other concerns (claim, cancel, cooldown, queries, dependency, archive) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Changes to the state machine itself (statuses, transitions) — defined in TD-027 §1–§2; not modified here.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 6 lifecycle Operation types listed in PROPOSED FIX. Each implements `Apply(b *Bead) error`. Test: `TestOperation_LifecycleOps_AllImplementApply` (reflection-enumerated).
2. `cli/internal/bead/ops/lifecycle/` package exists with the helper functions. Each helper has at least one test against `*Store`: `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_HappyPath`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Transition_RejectsInvalidTransition`, `TestOpsLifecycle_CloseWithEvidence_RecordsProvenance`, `TestOpsLifecycle_Reopen_FromClosedFails` (closed is terminal), `TestOpsLifecycle_ParkToProposed_AppendsTriageEvent`.
3. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 lifecycle methods. Compile-time check: `grep -E 'func \(s \*Store\) (TransitionLifecycle|SetLifecycleStatus|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus|CloseWithEvidence|AppendNotes|Reopen|ParkToProposed)\b' cli/internal/bead/*.go` returns nothing.
4. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store`. Verified by `grep -rln 'store\.\(TransitionLifecycle\|SetLifecycleStatus\|UpdateWithLifecycleStatus\|CloseWithEvidence\|AppendNotes\|Reopen\|ParkToProposed\)' cli/` outside the bead package and test fixtures, which returns zero matches.
5. Existing test behavior preserved: `cli/internal/bead/store_test.go`, `chaos_test.go`, `backend_conformance_test.go` continue to pass (they test storage primitives, not the workflow helpers).
6. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

  After the sister implementation bead removes network I/O from Land(),
  Land() is local-only: worktree-merge + update-ref + evidence commit
  under a brief local lock, with no fetch or push. A worker that crashes
  mid-Land (between UpdateRefTo and the next bead's claim) can leave the
  tracker lock held by a dead process. The next worker that starts must
  break the stale lock and recover cleanly. There is no test for this
  local-only recovery path today.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:196-218 breakStaleTrackerLock removes
    the lock if the owner PID is dead.
  - No test exercises the scenario where the dead owner had completed
    UpdateRefTo (advancing local main) but did not have a chance to
    perform any follow-up tracker writes before exiting.
  - With network I/O removed from Land(), the only stale-lock-recovery
    scenario worth covering is the local-only one: worker died after
    advancing local ref; next worker breaks lock, sees local-ahead state,
    proceeds.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add one chaos test in cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go (or a new
  file cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go):

  TestTrackerLock_StaleLockAfterPartialLocalLand:
    - Simulate a worker that acquired withMainGitLock, called
      UpdateRefTo successfully (local main advanced), then was killed
      without releasing the lock.
    - Next worker arrives, calls breakStaleTrackerLock at
      tracker_lock.go:196-218, observes the lock is held by a dead PID,
      breaks it.
    - Next worker proceeds with its own Land() and asserts it sees the
      local-ahead state (does not re-merge the previous worker's work,
      does not roll back).

  Use a per-test sandbox (testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir()) and
  channel-based barriers / synctest for deterministic timing — no
  wall-clock sleeps in CI.

NON-SCOPE
  - The sister implementation bead handles the network-I/O removal.
  - Push-race or fetch-race scenarios are obsolete after the sister
    implementation bead lands; they no longer exist in single-node mode.
  - Multi-machine coordination chaos coverage is a future federation
    concern.","id":"ddx-c019e48a","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:test","prevention"],"priority":2,"status":"open","title":"agent/chaos: cover stale-lock recovery after partial-land (PreClaimFetch race)","updated_at":"2026-05-09T19:53:25.493582886Z"}

- Add a worktree_lost scenario proving the bead remains retryable and evidence/diagnostics survive.
- Use temp dirs and fakes/injectable hooks; do not fill real disks or call live model providers.

NON-SCOPE

`ddx work` without flags produces `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate for pins min_power=7 max_power=8: N candidates rejected` because the pre-dispatch lint hook inherits `rcfg.Profile()="default"`, and fizeau's "default" profile pins `min_power=7,max_power=8`. Operators whose providers fall outside that band — local providers (power<=6) or frontier cloud providers via openrouter (power=9) — see every candidate rejected and the agent exits in <1s with no_evidence_produced. The post-attempt triage hook has the same latent bug. Pre-claim intake already sidesteps this with `ClearProfile: true`. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` will emit the dispatch-failure event on the next claimed bead — see `.ddx/executions/20260508T173611-c6069449/manifest.json` events for a captured instance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:11` normalizes empty profile to `"default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:186-189` flows `rcfg.Profile()` into `ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile` when no `ProfileOverride` is set and `ClearProfile` is false.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132` constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` without `ClearProfile` or `ProfileOverride`, so the lint hook's request carries `Profile="default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` has the same omission.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:96-106` demonstrates the fix shape: `ClearProfile: true`.
- Downstream: fizeau's `internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml` pins the `default` profile to `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`; `service_routing.go` `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays that on every request whose `Profile` field is set.

PROPOSED FIX
- In `NewPreDispatchLintHook` (`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132`), set `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`. Lint is a mechanical scoring task; no profile constraint is needed.
- In `NewPostAttemptTriageHook` (`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118`), set `ClearProfile: true` for the same reason.
- Do not change `executeOnService`, `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, or any caller that legitimately uses Profile="default" with explicit `--harness/--model` overrides (those bypass the profile bound via fizeau's `routePowerBoundsForRequest`).
- Do not touch `NewPreClaimIntakeHook`; it already does the right thing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` or the meaning of "default" — execute-bead and `ddx run` callers still rely on it as the default request shape and supply their own `--harness`/`--model`/`--profile` to bypass the bound.
- Do not modify fizeau's catalog.
- Do not add a new global setting; the fix is local to the two hook constructors.

The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel errors.
4. `cli/internal/bead/context.go` — typed accessors `WithIdentity`/`IdentityFromContext`/`WithTrace`/`TraceFromContext`. Package docstring per TD-027 §9 enumerating allowed ctx values.
5. `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` (expanded) — declare `BeadInitializer`, `BeadReader`, `BeadLifecycle` (Create + Apply only at this stage), `BeadEventReader`, `BeadEventWriter` interfaces. All ctx-first. Event interfaces are declarations only in this bead; no `*Store` event compile-time assertions until E. Composite `Backend` is not added yet (other sub-interfaces missing until per-concern beads land). `OperationApplier` optional interface declared. `RawBackend` retained with new docstring warning new backends off whole-corpus shape.

## Modify *Store

6. Add `*Store.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` method per TD-027 §7.2: type-asserts wrapped `RawBackend` to `OperationApplier` and delegates; otherwise generic `WithLock + ReadAll + op.Apply + WriteAll`.
7. Add ctx as first parameter to `*Store.Init`, `ReadAll`, `ReadAllFiltered`, `Get`, `GetWithArchive`, `Create`, `Update`, `Close`, plus `GenID`. Do not change `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind` here; E owns event ctx threading.

## Modify JSONLBackend

8. Add `JSONLBackend.Apply(ctx, id, op Operation) error` — satisfies `OperationApplier`. Empty type-switch + generic fallback (the per-concern Claim bead adds the `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` sidecar-optimization cases later).

## Caller migration (ctx threading only — scoped to foundational non-event methods)

9. Update every caller in `cli/cmd/`, `cli/internal/agent/`, `cli/internal/server/`, `cli/internal/escalation/`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/`, `cli/internal/attemptmetrics/`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/`, `cli/internal/exec/`, `cli/internal/agent/try/` that calls the foundational non-event methods (Init/ReadAll/Get/Create/Update/Close/GenID) to pass `ctx` through. Use `cmd.Context()` for cobra commands; existing request ctx for HTTP/GraphQL handlers; `context.Background()` only where no ctx is naturally available.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event method ctx threading (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) — E bead `ddx-f39b41b3`.
- Helper packages (`ops/<concern>/`) — per-concern beads handle these.
- Removing any `*Store` workflow methods (Claim, Heartbeat, TransitionLifecycle, RequestCancel, SetExecutionCooldown, etc.) — per-concern beads handle removal as they migrate callers.
- AxonStore implementation — separate bead `ddx-9c5bca8f`.
- Caller narrowing from `*bead.Store` field types to `bead.Backend` interface — happens in BL bead `ddx-74452926` after per-concern beads complete.
- Module boundary lockdown (relocation to `internal/storage/`) — BL bead.
- Schema-versioning migration framework — deferred until first breaking change.

The core queue queries still implement lifecycle policy with local conditionals: ready filtering excludes lifecycle labels, blocked means unsatisfied dependencies, status counts only cover a subset of statuses, and needs-human has a dedicated query lane. - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1575-1719 classifies dependency waits, status=blocked, needs_human, needs_investigation, cooldowns, ineligible, superseded, and epics in one store-specific function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1804-1867 counts Open/Closed/Ready/Blocked/NeedsHuman but not the six lifecycle statuses.

PROPOSED FIX
Refactor store ready, execution-ready, dependency-waiting, externally-blocked, operator-attention, and status-summary queries to call the pure lifecycle state machine. Ready/work eligibility must be status=open with satisfied dependencies, no active claim, and no cooldown/ineligible/superseded/epic-exclusion facts. Dependency waiting must remain status=open and appear as a derived waiting bucket, not status=blocked. status=blocked must represent external recheckable blockers only. status=proposed replaces NeedsHuman for operator-attention queries. closed satisfies dependents; cancelled does not.

NON-SCOPE

When readiness returns actionable_but_rewritten but the rewrite validator rejects the proposed mutation, ddx work logs a blocked outcome and unclaims the bead without parking it. In continuous mode, the same bead can be selected again immediately, producing a loop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:767-783 handles `PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten` validation errors by emitting `pre_claim_intake.blocked`, calling `Unclaim`, and continuing. It does not call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection`.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:798-821 correctly parks other terminal non-actionable intake outcomes with `needs_human`; the unsafe rewrite rejection branch diverged from that policy.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_intake_test.go:383-433 verifies unsafe rewrites do not execute, but does not assert `needs_human`, `intake.blocked` bead evidence, or removal from `ReadyExecution`.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1508-1511 already excludes `needs_human` beads from `ReadyExecution`, so parking is sufficient to stop the loop.

PROPOSED FIX
- In the unsafe rewrite rejection branch, call `parkBeadPostIntakeRejection` with outcome `ambiguous_needs_human` and the validator detail before unclaiming.
- Change operator wording from `releasing` to `parking for human review` or equivalent.
- Preserve the validator failure detail in the event stream and bead evidence.
- Strengthen tests so rejected rewrites are proven to be open/unclaimed, labeled `needs_human`, recorded with `intake.blocked`, and absent from `ReadyExecution`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the validated replacement rewrite policy; that is ddx-a41e6fbb.
- Do not change readiness routing or model selection.
- Do not change generic ambiguous_needs_human parking semantics outside this branch.

  TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle in
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195 passes
  3/3 in isolation but fails reliably during drain-induced load (e.g.,
  observed twice during the 2026-05-09 drain at 19:22:43 and 19:30:52
  while the drain was processing ddx-94a368b6 with concurrent test
  workloads on the same machine).

  Failure surfaces as `--- FAIL: TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNext…`
  during lefthook pre-push full-tests; pushes get rejected and the
  drain self-recovers via push-race retry, wasting one round of
  full-tests (~168s).

  Note: after sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 drops full-tests from pre-push,
  this flake stops blocking drain pushes (it would only fire in CI).
  But the underlying flake is still real and should be fixed for CI
  reliability.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262.
  The test wires:
    - A worker in watch mode with IdleInterval=time.Hour.
    - A buffered progressCh listener goroutine that, on loop.idle,
      creates a new ready bead and sends to wakeCh.
    - The worker is expected to pick up the new bead and the executor
      callback cancels context; the test then asserts the stdout log
      contains a "taking next ready bead" transition line.

  The flake mode is goroutine scheduling under contention:
    - Under heavy load (e.g., a parallel drain + lefthook full-tests),
      the goroutine listening on progressCh can be delayed beyond the
      worker's loop iteration window.
    - The worker may exit watch mode (via its context cancel from a
      different code path) before the goroutine has a chance to send
      the wake signal, so the "next ready bead" transition log line
      is never emitted.
    - Or: the wake signal is sent but the worker's select-loop picks a
      different branch first under load.

  The test also depends on a fixed-time injector (`fixedWatchStdoutTime`)
  for its log-line assertion, which assumes precise ordering between
  the test's bead creation and the worker's output. Under load, that
  ordering can flip without invalidating the actual behavior.

PROPOSED FIX
  Investigate cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_stay_alive_test.go:195-262
  with the goal of removing load-sensitivity:

  1. Replace the goroutine-listener pattern with a deterministic
     synchronization barrier — e.g., the test could explicitly wait
     for the worker to enter loop.idle (via a synchronous channel
     receive from progressCh) before creating the new bead, rather
     than handling the event in a separate goroutine.

  2. If the test must stay async, increase the buffer on progressCh
     and wakeCh so a slow goroutine doesn't drop signals; assert
     post-hoc on the buffered channel contents rather than racing the
     worker's exit.

  3. Use t.Parallel() if appropriate, but only after confirming the
     test's t.Setenv / store fixtures are parallel-safe.

  4. As a minimum: add a t.Helper()-decorated retry wrapper for the
     log-line assertion so a load-induced miss can re-poll briefly,
     OR mark the test as testing.Short()-skip if it cannot be made
     deterministic (NOT preferred — fix it properly).

NON-SCOPE
  - Refactoring the watch-mode logging system itself (the production
    behavior is correct; only the test's assertion harness is flaky).
  - Other flaky tests elsewhere in the suite.
  - Adding a load-simulation test (separate concern).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-f1c476c3 (drop full-tests from pre-push) reduces
    the urgency of this fix because the flake no longer blocks drain
    pushes — but it still blocks CI on every push to origin.","events_attachment":"ddx-dc691319/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-dc691319","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","kind:fix","observed-failure"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"11","session_id":"eb-6948a779","status":"closed","title":"fix(agent): TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle flakes under load","updated_at":"2026-05-10T16:04:56.493740726Z"}

DDx synthesizes a `default` routing profile for any operator who does not pass `--profile`, then sends `Profile="default"` to fizeau. Fizeau's `default` profile (`fizeau/internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:438-443`) hard-pins `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`. For operators whose model catalog has no power-7-or-8 entries — e.g., local providers (power 5–6) plus frontier cloud (power 9) — `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays those bounds and the routing engine rejects every candidate as `below_min_power` or `above_max_power`. Result: zero-config `ddx work` claims a bead, dispatches with empty intent, fizeau returns "no viable routing candidate," and the agent exits in <1s with `no_evidence_produced`. Power was meant to be a hint, not a gate; synthesizing a constraint the operator never asked for inverts that policy. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` triggers the dispatch-failure on hooks today and would trigger it on the implementation dispatch too if explicit `--harness/--model` flags weren't bypassing the bound via fizeau's exact-pin escape (`routePowerBoundsForRequest`).

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:9-15` returns the constant `"default"` whenever the operator-supplied profile is empty.
- `cli/cmd/work.go:70` registers the `--profile` flag with default value `agent.DefaultRoutingProfile`, so cobra fills in `"default"` even when the operator omits the flag.
- `cli/cmd/try.go:86` does the same for `ddx try`.
- `cli/cmd/run.go:137`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:298`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1613`, `cli/cmd/try.go:215`, and `cli/internal/server/workers.go:{303,409,705}` all call `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, inheriting the synthesis.
- Downstream fizeau gating: `routePowerPolicyForRequest` (`fizeau/service_routing.go:1117-1141`) returns zero bounds only when `req.Profile == ""`; any non-empty profile string causes a catalog lookup that overlays its `min_power`/`max_power` onto the request.

PROPOSED FIX
- Change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to return the trimmed input as-is; an empty operator value stays empty. Keep the function (callers want trim+normalize), drop the synthesis branch.
- Remove the `DefaultRoutingProfile = "default"` constant, or repurpose it strictly for the help-text label rather than a runtime default. Update `cli/cmd/work.go:70` and `cli/cmd/try.go:86` so the cobra flag's default is `""` and the help string reads "Routing profile: cheap, fast, smart (empty = unconstrained, let the agent service choose)".
- Audit every caller of `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to confirm they tolerate `""` (today they all forward the value into `CLIOverrides.Profile` or a workers spec — empty is already valid downstream).
- After this change, zero-config `ddx work` sends `Profile=""` → fizeau applies no profile-derived power bound → routing picks any available model regardless of power tier, honoring the "power is a hint" policy.
- The pre-dispatch lint and post-attempt triage hooks (`lint_hook.go`, `triage_hook.go`) still need `ClearProfile: true` so they ignore an explicitly operator-set `--profile fast`/`--profile standard` (those profiles also pin power 7-8); that defensive change is tracked in ddx-c54b817c and remains correct.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change fizeau's `default` profile or its power-gating semantics; making power bounds advisory rather than hard is a fizeau-side change and out of scope here.
- Do not change `--min-power`/`--max-power` flag handling. When the operator explicitly asks for a power bound, honor it; this bead only stops DDx from inventing one.
- Do not remove `cheap`/`fast`/`smart`/`standard` profile values — they remain available as explicit operator opt-ins.
- Do not modify ddx-c54b817c's hook-level fix; it is independently defensive against operator-set profiles.

DDx server workers still execute bead work in-process instead of spawning the canonical ddx work command. That leaves an alternate execution path inside the server process, duplicates worker lifecycle behavior, and weakens both ADR-022's worker/server boundary and the DDx/Fizeau execution boundary.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:735-969 builds server-local execution configuration and runs bead execution inline instead of delegating to a ddx work subprocess.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go uses BeadWorkerFactory as a production seam around the in-process executor, so server worker behavior can diverge from the installed ddx work CLI.
- Previous attempts parked on missing unified ExecuteLoopSpec wiring; the cleanup still needs to make the subprocess contract explicit and preserve MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, OpaquePassthrough, and FromRev.

## Why this attempt declined to commit

The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package.

### Actual surface (vs stated estimate)

cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together:

- in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction
- per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization
- in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer`
- in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation)
- in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService)
- per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net
- progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress`
- watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS
- terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result
- BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes

Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via:

- /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress
- PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial)
- worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging
- Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent

### Test surface

cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:

- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or
- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).

This is the bulk of the work, not "test updates."

### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)

"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit"

Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper.

### Size-check verdict

- More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables.
- AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate).
- More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes.

The bead's "~200 LOC + test updates" estimate (echoing the ADR-022 step 7 roadmap line) materially understates the work.

## Proposed decomposition (orchestrator should create from main repo)

Create the following children of ddx-eccc6efb from the **main repo** (not from an execution worktree — IDs created from a worktree get the worktree dirname as the project prefix and become unusable):

### 7a — runWorker exec rewrite + parent-side subprocess plumbing
Title: ADR-022 step 7a: workers.go runWorker → exec ddx work subprocess (progress via /api/workers/{id}/event, watchdog via PID)
Scope:
- Replace the in-process `worker.Run(...)` body in cli/internal/server/workers.go with `exec.CommandContext` of `ddx work` (resolved via os.Executable() + arg-vec flags + DDX_PROJECT_ROOT env).
- Record child PID on WorkerRecord; ensure watchdog SIGTERM/SIGKILL via PID still works.
- Bridge child-emitted progress events into the existing parent SSE subscriber path — either by POST to /api/workers/{id}/event being multiplexed back to the in-memory progressCh, or by tailing the child's worker-events.jsonl.
- Stop()/cancel/wake plumbing: Stop signals the PID; wakeCh is replaced by sending a SIGUSR1 (or equivalent) handled by ddx work, OR documented as no-op when subprocess.
- Drop in-process LandCoordinator coupling, in-process DefaultBeadReviewer, in-process CostCapTracker, in-process RoutePreflight from this code path.
- Update workers_test.go, workers_costcap_test.go, workers_gates_test.go, workers_watchdog_test.go, workers_wake_test.go, workers_reap_unix.go, workers_stop_test.go, workers_stop_propagation_test.go, workers_e2e_test.go, graphql_subscription_worker_test.go to use a subprocess-stub binary (or test-binary reentry) instead of BeadWorkerFactory injection.
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker untouched at this point — still uses ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec.
Acceptance: subprocess exec path works; existing test scenarios pass against subprocess-stub; lefthook pre-commit green.
Estimated: ~800–1,000 LOC.

### 7b — handler migration + ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec deletion
Title: ADR-022 step 7b: handleStartExecuteLoopWorker → exec ddx work; delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec
Scope:
- handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes HTTP request → builds argv/env → calls a thinner StartExecuteLoop(opts) that no longer takes the legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct.
- Delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec struct and its JSON marshalling.
- Update server_test.go, bounded_egress_test.go, federation_*_test.go, and any other call sites that constructed an ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal.
Acceptance: spec struct gone; handler tests assert exec form; full ./internal/server/... + ./cmd/... green.
Estimated: ~300–500 LOC.

### 7c — End-to-end integration test (scripted-claude-stub or opt-in real claude)
Title: ADR-022 step 7c: end-to-end test — server-spawned worker via exec path registers, invokes harness on fixture bead, lands commit
Scope: Build the AC #5 evidence the parent bead requires:
- Spin up a server.Server in-test bound to an ephemeral port.
- Use scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh (federated profile) for a clean ddx-initialized git repo with one ready bead.
- POST /api/agent/workers/execute-loop with --once.
- Assert the child ddx work process registers via POST /api/workers/register, claims the bead, the scripted-claude-stub (matching the same exec args + env-var path real claude uses) emits an evidence file, the attempt commits, and the bead closes (or preserves with a known-good status).
- Optional: gate a separate test on `DDX_E2E_REAL_CLAUDE=1` that runs the same scenario against a real `claude` binary on $PATH.
Acceptance: green CI run with scripted-claude-stub end-to-end; opt-in real-claude variant documented.
Estimated: ~400–600 LOC + fixture infra.

### Dependencies
- 7b dep 7a (handler migration assumes runWorker already speaks exec)
- 7c dep 7b (end-to-end via the new exec path requires the new handler shape)

## What blocked this attempt from a clean partial commit

The 6 ACs are tightly interlocking — partial implementation (e.g. just runWorker rewrite without handler migration, or rewrite without test sweep) leaves the package in a non-compiling or red-test state (BeadWorkerFactory injection points either still exist and are dead, or are removed and break tests). A clean intermediate slice requires the explicit decomposition above so each child commits a green tree.

## Bookkeeping

A child bead was inadvertently created from this worktree before the orchestrator-must-be-main-repo issue was identified; its ID `.execute-bead-wt-ddx-eccc6efb-20260503T065047-61e4ed20-c4de2d6e` is malformed and the bead has been closed. The orchestrator should disregard it and create the three children above from the main repo where the project prefix resolves to `ddx-`.

At least 3 caller files have field types narrowed from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadEventReader` or `bead.BeadEventWriter` (or composite) — concrete examples: `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go` (uses `EventsByKind` + `AppendEvent`; narrow to `BeadEventReader + BeadEventWriter`). Remaining caller narrowing happens in BL.

7. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:57.463979704Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f39b41b3","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:57Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 event-log methods (`AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind`) used widely across the agent/server/cmd layers. Per TD-027 §6 these belong on `BeadEventReader` and `BeadEventWriter` sub-interfaces; per TD-027 every interface method takes `ctx context.Context` first. The Foundation bead declares those interfaces but deliberately leaves `*Store`'s event methods unchanged so event caller migration remains a focused, testable slice here.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/bead/events*.go` define `AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error`, `Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)`, `EventsByKind(id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error)` — no ctx. After the Foundation bead, `BeadEventReader` and `BeadEventWriter` interfaces have ctx-first signatures, but `*Store` does not satisfy them until this bead updates the event methods and callers.

# PROPOSED FIX
1. Update `*Store.AppendEvent`, `Events`, `EventsByKind` to take `ctx context.Context` as first parameter.
2. Add compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadEventReader = (*Store)(nil)` and `var _ BeadEventWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Migrate callers that hold `*Store` and use event methods — thread ctx through the existing call sites:
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go`, `bead_evidence.go` (operator evidence commands)
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go`, `resolver_mutation_beads.go`, `resolver_sub_bead.go`
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` (event appends on state transitions)
   - Other event-emitting paths
4. Where the caller narrows naturally to event-only access (e.g., a resolver that only reads/appends events), update the field type from `*bead.Store` to `bead.BeadEventReader` or `bead.BeadEventWriter` (or a composite). This is opportunistic narrowing; full narrowing pass happens in BL.

# NON-SCOPE
- Event vocabulary (`kind` values) is documented in TD-027 §13; not modified here.
- Event externalization to attachments (TD-027 §17) — separate concern; not modified.
- Non-event foundational method ctx threading — F bead `ddx-c6317784`.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, claim, etc.) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Labels
phase:iterate, area:bead, area:events, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027

# Parent + Deps

.ddx/beads.jsonl:392:{"acceptance":"1. TestTrackerCommit_MalformedRegularFileLockRecovery creates an old regular file at `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`, proves acquisition removes it with single-path removal and commits tracker changes successfully.
2. TestTrackerCommit_MalformedFreshRegularFileFailsFast creates a fresh regular file at `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`, uses a policy whose retry budget would otherwise wait at least 1s, proves `withTrackerLockPolicy` returns a malformed-lock error in under 100ms, and proves the file remains in place.
3. TestTrackerCommit_MalformedSymlinkLockFailsFast creates a symlink at `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` when the platform permits symlink creation, proves acquisition fails fast, and proves the symlink target is not removed; skip this test when symlink creation is unsupported.
4. TestTrackerCommit_MissingOwnerDoesNotReportUnknown holds a real lock directory without a `pid` sidecar and proves timeout diagnostics say `owner pid: missing`, not `owner pid: unknown`.
5. TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery and TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy still pass, proving normal stale-directory recovery and real contention retry behavior are unchanged.
6. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestTrackerCommit_(Malformed|MissingOwner|StaleLockRecovery|RetryBackoffPolicy)' -count=1` passes.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"fdb7900567c9074100c31598b0aaf8da216b31b2","created_at":"2026-05-08T01:17:51.679143599Z","description":"PROBLEM
A malformed `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` path can stop `ddx work` before it claims any bead. The observed incident was a zero-byte regular file at `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`; `ddx work` then printed repeated lines like `pre-claim hook: tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, owner pid: unknown) (skipping ddx-...)` for every ready candidate.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:130 calls `os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)` and treats every failure as ordinary lock contention.
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:139-149 only calls `breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir)` and then sleeps/retries. That stale path only understands a lock directory with `pid` and `acquired_at` sidecars, so a regular file at the lock path is never classified or removed.
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:156-161 reports `owner pid: unknown` by trying to read `lockDir/pid`, which is impossible when `lockDir` is a regular file.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:499-522 now specifies the required filesystem-shape contract: `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` is a lock directory; malformed non-directories must be classified before retry/backoff and must not masquerade as `owner pid: unknown` contention.

PROPOSED FIX
- In cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go, classify `os.Mkdir(lockDir)` failures immediately using `os.Lstat(lockDir)`.
- Only enter retry/backoff after the existing path is confirmed to be a directory that represents real lock contention.
- Preserve existing directory stale-lock recovery for old `pid`/`acquired_at` sidecars.
- If `Lstat` shows the lock path disappeared after the failed mkdir, retry acquisition immediately.
- If `Lstat` shows an exact regular file older than `trackerLockStaleAge`, remove it with `os.Remove` only, then retry acquisition immediately.
- If `Lstat` shows a fresh regular file, fail fast with a malformed-lock diagnostic that names `.ddx/.git-tracker.lock` and says a lock directory was expected.
- If `Lstat` shows a symlink, socket, device, or other special file, fail fast and do not remove it.
- Update owner diagnostics so real lock-directory timeouts report `owner pid: <pid>` or `owner pid: missing`; malformed paths must not fall through to `owner pid: unknown`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change the lock path.
- Do not add a new bead status, label, worker state, or event kind.
- Do not change claim semantics, queue ranking, or pre-claim hook policy.
- Do not use `os.RemoveAll` for malformed non-directory paths.
- Do not remove symlinks or special filesystem objects.
- Do not mutate the active server pre-claim hook bead ddx-f6fd75e7; this is a sibling lock primitive hardening bead.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-5cb6e6cd. No deps.
INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-da11a34a introduced the main-git/tracker lock and retry policy; this bead hardens that primitive against malformed ignored lock paths.
- ddx-f6fd75e7 is an active sibling about server pre-claim error propagation. This bead must not expand into that server policy work.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"","created_at":"2026-05-08T03:58:28.665998467Z","kind":"lifecycle_reconciled","source":"ddx bead reconcile","summary":"closed bead has stale no_changes lifecycle metadata"}],"events_attachment":"ddx-f812c929/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-f812c929","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:git","kind:bug","reliability","prevention","observed-failure","spec:TD-031","spec:FEAT-010","reconciled:no-changes-state"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":0,"spec-id":"TD-031","status":"closed","title":"agent: recover malformed tracker lock paths","updated_at":"2026-05-08T03:58:28.666000884Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:397:{"acceptance":"1. `ddx work --help` documents `--watch` and `--idle-interval` as the primary long-running worker surface, with examples for `ddx work`, `ddx work --once`, `ddx work --watch`, and `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 15s`.
2. `ddx work --help` and generated CLI docs no longer contain `--poll-interval`; `ddx work --poll-interval=30s` fails with an unknown-flag error.
3. `ddx work` with no watch flag keeps drain-and-exit behavior on an empty execution-ready queue; `TestWorkDefaultDrainModeExitsOnEmptyQueue` covers the default.
4. `ddx work --watch` keeps running across an empty queue until context cancellation or signal; `TestWorkWatchModeStaysAliveWithEmptyQueue` covers the watch path.
5. `ddx work --once` still processes at most one ready bead and exits; existing `TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits` remains green.
6. `ddx work --once --watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--once` and `--watch` are mutually exclusive.
7. `ddx work --idle-interval 15s` without `--watch` returns a usage error explaining that `--idle-interval` only applies in watch mode.
8. Server-managed workers created through REST and GraphQL default to watch mode with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly selects once/drain behavior. New request/input shapes use `mode` and `idle_interval`, not `poll_interval`.
9. The loop/runtime no longer relies on raw `PollInterval > 0` as the only semantic mode test; mode and idle interval are represented explicitly at the command/runtime boundary.
10. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run 'TestWork(DefaultDrain|Watch|Once|IdleInterval|PollIntervalRemoved)|TestLoop_(StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue|OnceFlagStillExits|ExplicitDrainExits)|TestStartExecuteLoop'` passes.
11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-09T09:33:49Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"310168","closing_commit_sha":"7f329d7c4b702bb064fcf33662a8060e2325be2e","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.498901669Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f9acb86d","depends_on_id":"ddx-7d9e1e82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-08T13:37:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
`ddx work` currently uses `--poll-interval` as the public way to request a long-running worker. That name is ambiguous because drain mode also scans the queue between attempts; the real operator choice is whether the process exits when execution-ready work dries up or keeps watching for newly arriving work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/work.go:75 defines `--poll-interval` with default 0 and documents it as the continuous scanning control.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:649-682 treats `PollInterval > 0` as the semantic switch from drain-and-exit to idle/watch behavior.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2425-2437 and cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:112-124 default server-managed workers to a 30s poll interval, but the persisted/request vocabulary still names only the interval, not the worker mode.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md will define the intended vocabulary in ddx-7d9e1e82: `once`, `drain`, `watch`, and `idle interval`, with no `--poll-interval` compatibility alias.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement explicit watch/drain semantics in the CLI and server worker entry points:
- Add `ddx work --watch` for long-running behavior: drain ready work, then remain idle and rescan for newly arriving execution-ready beads.
- Add `ddx work --idle-interval <duration>` for the sleep between empty-queue scans in watch mode, defaulting to 30s when watch mode is active.
- Keep direct CLI `ddx work` as drain-and-exit unless `--watch` is supplied. `ddx work --once` remains the one-bead mode.
- Treat `--once` and `--watch` as incompatible and return a usage error when both are supplied.
- Treat `--idle-interval` without `--watch` as a usage error. The interval tunes watch mode; it does not implicitly select a mode.
- Remove `--poll-interval` from `ddx work` entirely. It is not retained as a compatibility alias. Help and generated docs must not list it.
- Replace new server-managed worker request/input vocabulary with `mode` (`once`, `drain`, `watch`) and `idle_interval`; do not accept `poll_interval` for new worker starts. Existing persisted worker records may be read best-effort if necessary, but this bead must not expose `poll_interval` as a supported input alias.
- Keep server-managed workers in watch mode by default with a 30s idle interval unless the request explicitly asks for once/drain semantics.
- Use one internal representation for mode + idle interval so callers do not branch directly on raw `PollInterval > 0`.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign route/provider/model passthrough.
- Do not change success/failure classification, bead closing policy, cost accounting, or retry cooldown rules.
- Do not implement final human summary wording; ddx-dc300be9 consumes this once loop state is explicit.
- Do not perform ExecuteLoopSpec unification; that remains owned by ddx-29058e2a and its server children.

Pinned `ddx work` workers are still producing repeated lifecycle-hook routing warnings before every attempt:

- `readiness route unavailable: runner error: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 3 candidates rejected`
- `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: policy "air-gapped" has no candidate satisfying local endpoint`

This is happening even when the worker is explicitly started with a viable implementation route, for example:

`ddx work --watch --idle-interval 30s --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.5`

The main implementation attempt then routes correctly to `codex/gpt-5.4-mini`, but pre-claim intake and pre-dispatch lint ignore the operator's pins and spend 60-120 seconds failing through unrelated unpinned/profile routing. This slows every worker cycle and makes healthy pinned execution look system-unready.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:134-143` constructs `AgentRunRuntime` with `ProfileOverride: selectProfileForDispatch(... SelectStrongestProfile)` and sets `ClearRoutingPins: true`, `ClearProfile: true`, `ClearMinPower: true`, and `ClearMaxPower: true`. That strips explicit worker harness/model pins before dispatching the intake prompt.

`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-137` does the same for pre-dispatch lint, selecting the cheapest profile and setting `ClearRoutingPins: true`, `ClearProfile: true`, `ClearMinPower: true`, and `ClearMaxPower: true`.

In projects where autorouting/local endpoint validation is not yet complete, those unpinned hook dispatches fail even though the pinned implementation route is usable.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update lifecycle hook dispatch so explicit operator route pins from `ResolvedConfig` are preserved for pre-claim intake and pre-dispatch lint when `ddx work`/`ddx try` was started with `--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`, or `--model-ref`.
- Keep the existing profile-selection behavior only for genuinely unpinned workers.
- Preserve the strong/cheap intent for unpinned hook dispatch without overriding explicit operator constraints.
- Ensure lifecycle hook failures caused by unavailable optional hook routes are not recorded as system-unready when the implementation route itself is explicitly pinned and executable.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement DDx-side model/provider/harness ranking, fuzzy matching, fallback, or catalog lookup.
- Do not change Fizeau routing policy.
- Do not remove lifecycle hooks.
- Do not change post-attempt review model routing.
- Do not change local provider validation policy beyond preserving explicit operator pins for these hook dispatches.

INTERSECTIONS
- This is a DDx-side workaround needed while Fizeau autorouter/local-provider validation work is in flight.
- Related to the Fizeau routing-boundary cleanup: DDx should pass explicit operator constraints through, not substitute a DDx-side route.","id":"ddx-011d5acd","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:execution","area:fizeau","kind:fix","prevention","routing-boundary"],"parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"work: preserve explicit route pins for lifecycle hook dispatch","updated_at":"2026-05-12T01:09:24.611283072Z"}

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified a gap in the
  operator playbook: when running multiple ddx work --once shells in
  parallel (after ddx-1f774edf lands), operators must understand that
  using the same harness across all N workers will throttle on the
  per-account rate limit (e.g., Anthropic 5-tier rate limits for the
  claude harness). Mixing harnesses across workers (claude + codex +
  openrouter, etc.) dodges per-harness ceilings and delivers true N×
  throughput. There is no operator documentation today that says this.

CURRENT STATE
  - CLAUDE.md describes the project structure and per-CLI commands but
    not the operator playbook for parallel drains.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/ contains reliability principles and alignment
    reviews but no operator-facing parallel-drain guide.
  - .agents/skills/ddx/reference/ and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/
    contain skill documentation but not an explicit
    parallel-drain-playbook section.
  - CLAUDE.md memory note feedback_models.md says "prefer claude harness
    for execute-loop; avoid agent harness for real work" — the harness
    selection guidance exists for single-worker drain but not for
    parallel.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/ if
  preferred for separation of concerns) titled "Parallel drain
  operator playbook" covering:

  1. When to use parallel drain: after ddx-1f774edf lands; not before
     (lock contention defeats the parallelism today).

  2. How to start: open N tmux panes / shells, each running a
     `ddx work --once` loop. No code change required.

  3. Harness mixing: example of running claude on one shell, codex on
     another, openrouter on a third — mixing dodges per-harness rate
     limits and avoids serializing on Anthropic quota.

  4. Cost ceiling: each ddx work invocation accepts --max-cost; budget
     across N shells.

  5. Conflict expectations: parallel landing produces some preservation
     under load (covered by the chaos sister bead); set operator
     expectation that occasional preservation is normal and the
     preserved iteration refs can be merged manually.

  6. Observability: point at the lock-metrics sister bead's output
     channels (once landed) for debugging contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a `ddx work --parallel N` flag (deliberately YAGNI per
    plan review; external shells deliver the same throughput).
  - Server-spawned-worker design (ADR-022 step 7, separate work).
  - Harness-selection algorithm (single-worker memory note already
    covers default selection).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister beads: ddx-1f774edf (must land before parallel drain is
    safe), the chaos test bead, the lock-metrics bead.","events":[{"actor":"erik","body":"{"rationale":"","score":0,"suggested_fixes":null,"waivers_applied":null,"warning":"lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate: 3 candidates rejected"}","created_at":"2026-05-12T02:46:06.85555782Z","kind":"bead-quality.lint","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"warning score=0"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T02:45:01.136030592Z","id":"ddx-170bea78","issue_type":"chore","labels":["phase:6","area:docs","area:operator","kind:doc","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"9","status":"in_progress","title":"docs(operator): document harness-mixing for parallel ddx work shells","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:46:06.900361993Z"}

  After sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the
  drain becomes safe for external parallelism (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently). However, parallel workers will land their
  own bead's worktree against the same local main and may produce
  overlapping changes — multi-worker conflict frequency on Land()'s
  git merge step is unmeasured. The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus
  self-review identified this as a key risk: under high parallelism,
  preservation (resource_exhausted to iteration ref) could become the
  common path rather than the exception, defeating the gain.

  This bead adds chaos coverage for the parallel-landing case and a
  sentinel that fails if the preservation rate exceeds an acceptable
  threshold under simulated load.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go cover
    bead.Store-layer concurrency only.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go covers single-worker
    semantics.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a covers stale-lock recovery for a single
    worker's local-only land. It does not cover N>1 workers landing in
    parallel.
  - No test verifies what happens when worker A lands branch-A into
    main, then worker B (whose worktree was branched from pre-A main)
    tries to land branch-B with overlapping changes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new
  file, or extend tracker_lock_test.go):

  1. TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each with its own bead/worktree
       touching disjoint files.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: all three land on local main, in some order; no
       preservation; main contains all three commits.

  2. TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each touching the same file with
       different content.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: exactly one lands; the other two are preserved to
       iteration refs; the preserved branches are intact and contain
       their bead's full work.

  3. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded:
     - Spawn N worker goroutines (N=4) over M cycles (M=10), each with
       a randomized mix of overlapping/non-overlapping changes.
     - Assert: preservation rate is < 50% (i.e., at least half of
       attempts land cleanly). This is a smoke check that parallel
       drain is viable, not a hard SLA.

  Use deterministic timing (channels or synctest), not wall-clock.
  Use testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir() per test.
  Depends on sister bead ddx-1f774edf so the lock holds are sub-second
  (otherwise the test exercises today's contention bug, not the
  parallel-landing concern).

NON-SCOPE
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps that mirror the real merge logic).
  - Coordinating workers via a "land queue" abstraction (separate
    architectural concern; this bead establishes the baseline measurement
    against which any such work would be evaluated).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (depends on it landing first).
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/lock: instrument bead.Store
    WithLock wait time" — its metrics are referenced by these tests'
    assertions if available.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a complements this for the single-worker
    stale-lock case.","events_attachment":"ddx-42741260/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T02:35:01.729089895Z","id":"ddx-42741260","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:test","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"8","session_id":"eb-f18b8501","status":"closed","title":"agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain landing under shared-main contention","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:45:00.616545921Z"}

  After the sister implementation bead removes network I/O from Land(),
  Land() is local-only: worktree-merge + update-ref + evidence commit
  under a brief local lock, with no fetch or push. A worker that crashes
  mid-Land (between UpdateRefTo and the next bead's claim) can leave the
  tracker lock held by a dead process. The next worker that starts must
  break the stale lock and recover cleanly. There is no test for this
  local-only recovery path today.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:196-218 breakStaleTrackerLock removes
    the lock if the owner PID is dead.

343:{"created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56.47101434Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-e1c743d3","depends_on_id":"ddx-c6317784","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-11T19:58:56Z"}],"description":"# PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 7 claim/heartbeat methods (`Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh`). Workers in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go`, `cli/internal/server/workers.go`, and the bead CLI hold `*Store` concretely for these. Per TD-027 §12 + §14, claim semantics belong in `ops/claim/` helper + typed `Operation` values, with the storage primitive being `Apply(ctx, id, ClaimOp{...})`. JSONLBackend's sidecar-write optimization for heartbeats (per `cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go`) MUST be preserved through the optional `OperationApplier` interface added in Foundation.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Heartbeats are written via a sidecar (`claim_liveness.go`) to avoid corpus churn at high frequency. The Foundation bead added `*Store.Apply` + `JSONLBackend.Apply` with an empty type-switch + generic fallback. The sidecar optimization needs the `SetClaimHeartbeat` case wired into `JSONLBackend.Apply`'s switch.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §11.5 + §12 + §14 for the claim concern:

1. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `ClaimOp{Owner, Session, Worktree string}` — CAS via `Apply` error return; sets claim fields and `claim.at = time.Now()`. Returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` from `Apply(*Bead)` if claim is held by different owner.
   - `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner string}` — clears claim; returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` if RequireOwner non-empty and doesn't match.
   - `SetClaimHeartbeat{At time.Time}`, `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}` — heartbeat field mutators.
2. Wire `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat` and `ClearClaimHeartbeat` to use `writeClaimSidecar` / `removeClaimSidecar` (preserving today's optimization). All other ops fall through to generic load-mutate-save.
3. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/liveness.go`:
   - `Acquire(ctx, l BeadLifecycle, id string, opts AcquireOptions) error` — wraps `ClaimOp`.
   - `Release(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{}` (unconditional).
   - `ReleaseAs(ctx, l, id, requiredOwner string) error` — wraps `UnclaimOp{RequireOwner: requiredOwner}`.
   - `Touch(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `SetClaimHeartbeat{At: time.Now()}`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id string) error` — wraps `ClearClaimHeartbeat{}`.
   - `IsFresh(b *Bead, maxAge time.Duration) bool` — pure predicate.
4. Migrate callers from `*Store.Claim`/`ClaimWithOptions`/`Unclaim`/`Heartbeat`/`TouchClaimHeartbeat`/`RemoveClaimHeartbeat`/`ClaimHeartbeatFresh` to `ops/claim/` helpers:
   - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead*.go` (worker claim/heartbeat path)
   - `cli/internal/server/workers.go`
   - `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_mutation_beads.go`
   - `cli/cmd/bead.go` (operator `ddx bead update --claim`/`--unclaim`)
   - `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`, related agent files
5. Remove `*Store.Claim`, `ClaimWithOptions`, `Unclaim`, `Heartbeat`, `TouchClaimHeartbeat`, `RemoveClaimHeartbeat`, `ClaimHeartbeatFresh` (7 methods).

# NON-SCOPE
- Stale-claim sweep policy — TD-031 §6.2 owns that operational concern; storage exposes the field and helpers; sweep policy lives in the drain loop.
- Other concerns (lifecycle, cancel, cooldown, etc.) — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — bead `ddx-74452926`.
- Modifying claim semantics (TD-027 §12).

# Acceptance criteria

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares the 4 claim Operation types. `ClaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrAlreadyClaimed` on owner mismatch. `UnclaimOp.Apply` returns `ErrNotClaimedByOwner` when `RequireOwner` is set and doesn't match. Tests: `TestOperation_ClaimOp_RejectsConflict`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Honored`, `TestOperation_UnclaimOp_RequireOwner_Empty_Unconditional`.
2. `JSONLBackend.Apply` switch cases for `SetClaimHeartbeat`/`ClearClaimHeartbeat` write only the sidecar; main corpus untouched. Test: `TestJSONLBackend_Apply_SetClaimHeartbeat_UsesSidecar` (compare mtime of `beads.jsonl` before/after).
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/claim/` package exists with the helpers + tests: `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_Acquire_ReturnsErrAlreadyClaimed`, `TestOpsClaim_Release_HappyPath`, `TestOpsClaim_ReleaseAs_RequiresMatchingOwner`, `TestOpsClaim_Touch_BumpsHeartbeat`, `TestOpsClaim_IsFresh_RespectsMaxAge`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes the 7 claim/heartbeat methods. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package. Verified by grep.
6. Workers continue to claim, heartbeat, and unclaim beads correctly. Test: end-to-end smoke `TestWorkerLifecycle_ClaimHeartbeatUnclaim` exercising a fake worker through `ops/claim/` helpers.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Labels

  Use a per-test sandbox (t.TempDir() or testutils.NewFixtureRepo) plus a

The factory workflow needs to improve or split poor beads before implementation is attempted. Current queue work only covers a Step 0 prompt hint inside the implementer prompt, which is too late: an agent can claim an ambiguous or feature-sized bead, fail, and burn retry budget before the system improves the input.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:680 invokes PreDispatchLintHook before claim, but the hook is lint-oriented and does not expose the required intake outcomes actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, or intake_error.
- cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:86 constructs NewPreDispatchLintHook, which emits lint results but does not safely rewrite bead text or create child beads.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1386 embeds Step 0 decomposition instructions in the implementer prompt, so decomposition happens after dispatch rather than before claim.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1399 still says auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2, while docs/triage/decomposition.md now defines the intake gate and default max_decomposition_depth policy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a PreClaimIntakeHook/guard that runs after ready selection and routing preflight but before Claim.
- Intake returns one of: actionable_atomic, actionable_but_rewritten, too_large_decomposed, ambiguous_needs_human, intake_error.
- For actionable_but_rewritten, mutate the bead only when the rewrite preserves intent, narrows wording, adds missing file/test references already inferable from repo evidence, and does not invent behavior or scope.
- For too_large_decomposed, create child beads with standalone descriptions/AC, dependency wiring, and a parent triage-decomposed event containing child_ids, rationale, and ac_map. The parent moves to blocked/decomposed instead of being executed.
- For ambiguous_needs_human, block with needs_human/triage:spec-gap labels and an operator-readable explanation.
- Intake errors fail open only when the hook infrastructure fails; a valid ambiguous/too-large decision must block/decompose, not proceed.
- No intake-only outcome counts as no-progress implementation failure.

The same anti-pattern likely exists in other ddx commands where flags travel cobra → function param → loose map → HTTP request struct → spec struct → consumer. Find them and either:
- File unification beads following the ExecuteLoopSpec pattern, OR
- Document them as already-clean (and why).

COMMANDS TO AUDIT (per codex review — expanded)

1. ddx agent run (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go ~395-414 cobra flags) PLUS its server endpoint /api/agent/run (cli/internal/server/server.go:2150-2188) AND its MCP equivalent (cli/internal/server/server.go:4537-4553). The server endpoint accepts only Harness/Prompt/Model/Effort while cobra has many more flags — same drop risk as ExecuteLoop, narrower fields.
2. ddx work passthrough (alias for ddx agent execute-loop with implicit OpaquePassthrough — covered by ddx-29058e2a but verify the implicit behavior is documented in the spec)
3. GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter (cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137) — covered by ddx-29058e2a explicitly, but audit any OTHER GraphQL adapters with similar patterns
4. Server-side worker variants in cli/internal/server/workers.go — anything else that takes a Spec struct
5. ddx bead create / update — flag set is small but check whether server endpoint mirrors
6. ddx server commands (ddx server workers list/show/log) — read-only, less risk but verify
7. ddx try (cli/cmd/try.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. The runTry function constructs config.CLIOverrides and ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in parallel to runWork's runAgentExecuteLoopImpl (see comment at try.go:155 acknowledging the duplication). Audit whether the same parallel-struct smell that plagues ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec also exists at the cobra-→-spec boundary for try, and if so file a follow-up unification bead. (Stale 2026-05-04 note "ddx try NOT shipped" was corrected 2026-05-07.)
8. ddx run (cli/cmd/run.go) — SHIPPED. INCLUDE in audit. Layer-1 invocation primitive; flags forward to Fizeau Execute. Lower drop-risk than try/work but verify cobra → service request shape parity.

AUDIT METHOD (per codex — generated diff, not gut-feel)

For each command:
1. Enumerate cobra flag list (programmatic: parse cobra command struct or grep for `cmd.Flags().*Var`).
2. Enumerate corresponding server JSON request struct fields (programmatic: AST parse the request struct in handler).
3. Enumerate the spec/persisted struct fields (programmatic).
4. Enumerate the final consumer fields (programmatic).
5. Diff each layer's field set; classify each field as: execution / control-plane / output-only / unsupported / dropped.
6. Drop-risk score: count of fields that exist in cobra but NOT in subsequent layers.

OUTPUT

Report at .ddx/executions/<run-id>/parallel-struct-audit.md:
- One section per command audited
- For each: layer count, list of fields per layer, classification table, drop-risk score, recommendation (unify / clean / N/A)
- Summary table: command | layer count | drop-risk count | recommendation

If unification candidates found, file follow-up beads using the ExecuteLoopSpec refactor (ddx-29058e2a) as the template.
