    "parent": "ddx-e34994e2",
    "description": "PROBLEM
ExecuteLoopSpec fields are declared in parallel structs across three independent code sites. When a flag or worker mode is added to cobra, the developer must also update the REST handler request struct in server.go and the GraphQL adapter request struct in graphql_adapters.go; missing one silently drops the field at runtime. The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.

ROOT CAUSE
Each execute-loop field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:

1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.

2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.

3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.

4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.

5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.

6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.

The consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.
- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.
- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.
- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.
- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Add parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) as the single cobra-to-spec conversion.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605: replace naked-local extraction with parseExecuteLoopSpec. Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Delete the parallel struct.
- cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409: delete the anonymous request struct in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker; decode directly into ExecuteLoopSpec. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.
- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. New field = automatic coverage.

NON-SCOPE
- ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7) owns replacing runWorker with exec ddx work; this bead does not change the subprocess vs in-process dispatch decision.
- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, MCP agent dispatch) may have the same smell; covered by sister bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string is owned by ddx-9d55601f; keep LabelFilter as string.

SEQUENCING
ddx-29058e2a (this bead) vs ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7 - server-spawn migration to exec ddx work): lands independently and before ddx-eccc6efb. ADR-022 says ddx-29058e2a remains valuable as worker-side struct unification (cobra to in-process flow) and should land independently. When ddx-eccc6efb lands later, it will delete ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec from workers.go as part of the exec migration; the unified ExecuteLoopSpec should already live in the durable agent package. If ddx-eccc6efb lands first, the scope of this bead narrows to the cobra and worker-facing layers that still exist.",
    "acceptance": "1. ExecuteLoopSpec struct defined in cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go. Exported fields include at minimum: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion. Audit: any field present in cobra extraction (agent_cmd.go:1582-1605) or workers.go:25-49 not in this list is a bug.

2. The unified spec does not contain a PollInterval field, a poll_interval JSON tag, or any public compatibility field for the removed polling flag. New CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.

3. parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd *cobra.Command) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions, error) exists in the new package. Called only from cobra RunE in agent_cmd.go. No parallel cobra-extraction block remains. DispatchOptions holds control-plane flags (--local, --json) and is not persisted to spec.json.

4. parseExecuteLoopSpec maps --once to Mode=once, maps --watch to Mode=watch, defaults to Mode=drain, maps --idle-interval to IdleInterval only for Mode=watch, and returns a usage error when --idle-interval is provided without --watch.

5. Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults() methods exist. Lifecycle enforced: (a) client calls Validate() before HTTP submit or inline dispatch; (b) server handleStartExecuteLoopWorker calls ApplyDefaults() then Validate() after decode, before StartExecuteLoop; (c) spec.json on disk is fully resolved (ApplyDefaults already ran at write time).

6. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2390) decodes directly into ExecuteLoopSpec; the anonymous request struct (currently lines 2395-2409) is deleted. Mode, IdleInterval, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, and FromRev survive the REST round-trip.

7. graphql_adapters.go anonymous request struct (lines 63-76) and its parallel StartExecuteLoop literal (lines 127-141) are deleted. GraphQL dispatch goes through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as the REST handler.

8. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec in cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is replaced by or aliased to ExecuteLoopSpec. spec.json files written by StartExecuteLoop use the unified struct and emit Mode plus IdleInterval.

9. runWorker (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658) consumes spec.MaxCostUSD as the cost-cap ceiling, with escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD fallback when zero. No hardcoded DefaultMaxCostUSD reference outside the fallback case.

10. OpaquePassthrough: server-submitted spec with OpaquePassthrough=true skips both validation gates - workers.go:357-361 (StartExecuteLoop pre-flight) and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch inside runWorker. TestStartExecuteLoop_OpaquePassthrough_SkipsValidation asserts that StartExecuteLoop with OpaquePassthrough=true does not call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService and returns a WorkerRecord without error even when the harness name is unresolvable.

11. Reflection round-trip test: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection in cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or executeloop sub-package. Uses reflect to iterate exported fields of ExecuteLoopSpec; for each field sets a non-zero sentinel value; marshals to JSON; unmarshals; asserts equality. Test fails if any exported field has a zero-value sentinel after unmarshal. A future added field automatically falls under coverage.

12. Backward compatibility for disk only: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ load without error. Files containing unknown legacy fields are tolerated and ignored by json.Unmarshal default behavior. Do not enable json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads.

13. Forward compatibility: SpecVersion int field present. Server returns HTTP 400 with a capability description when incoming SpecVersion > server-supported version. SpecVersion==0 (unset) is treated as the current version. TestHandleStartExecuteLoopWorker_HighSpecVersion_Returns400 covers this.

14. Regression: inline execution path behavior is unchanged except for the explicit once/drain/watch mapping. Existing tests that cover the inline path pass without modification.

15. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... green.

16. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

    "id": "ddx-3ee4ddcf",
    "title": "agent: replace legacy execute terminology with work/try nomenclature",
    "status": "open",
    "priority": 4,
    "issue_type": "task",
    "created_at": "2026-05-06T15:02:56.164866966Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-05-06T16:46:41.848356964Z",
    "labels": [
      "phase:2",
      "area:agent",
      "area:server",
      "area:docs",
      "kind:refactor",

    ],
    "parent": "ddx-5cb6e6cd",
    "description": "PROBLEM
The public DDx execution vocabulary is supposed to be `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`, but large parts of the codebase still use legacy `execute-bead` / `execute-loop` names in public help, comments, server worker kinds, GraphQL models, tests, docs, tracker metadata helpers, and internal symbols. Operators now see mixed terms and developers keep adding new code under the old names.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1485-1563 still defines the legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` cobra surface as if it were primary: help says "execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface", examples use `ddx agent execute-loop`, and deprecation text still names execute-loop rather than pointing to `ddx work`.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1590-1940 keeps the main work implementation named `runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`, `writeExecuteLoopResult`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopResult`, so new work-path changes naturally copy the old terminology.
- cli/cmd/try.go:213-333 implements the new `ddx try` command by constructing `ExecuteBeadExecutor`, `ExecuteBeadRuntime`, `ExecuteBeadWorker`, and `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, so the new public command still routes through legacy names.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:2233-2281 and cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts:343-397 expose worker kind/comment strings as `execute-loop` / `execute-bead`, which leaks legacy naming through API and UI fixtures.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:215-262 still describes attempt results and retry metadata as `execute-bead` / `execute-loop-*`, even while FEAT-010 and ADR-023 name the lifecycle `try` / `work`.
- Existing related beads are partially stale or narrower than this repo-wide cleanup: ddx-387a0178 owns C13 file/identifier renames and still says `ddx try` does not exist, while ddx-29058e2a / ddx-1a9cc01f still propose `ExecuteLoopSpec` naming for a work-side spec.

WHY THE NAMES DIVERGED
The codebase grew from the historical `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx agent execute-loop` commands. FEAT-010 introduced the clearer layered vocabulary (`run` = invocation atom, `try` = one bead attempt, `work` = queue drain), and `ddx try` / `ddx work` now exist as the intended operator surface. The implementation retained old symbols and compatibility aliases, so old names persisted as the easiest local pattern to copy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Perform a repo-wide terminology audit using `rg -n "execute-loop|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead"` and classify each hit as: public/user-facing rename, internal compatibility alias to retain, durable metadata key to retain with documentation, historical docs intentionally retained, or generated fixture/test data.
- Update public/user-facing CLI help, comments, API/GraphQL descriptions, server/frontend labels, tests, and docs to prefer `work` and `try`.
- Rename non-compatibility internal types/functions introduced by the FEAT-010 path from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary. Coordinate with ddx-387a0178 C13; if C13 already performs a rename, fold this cleanup into or after it rather than duplicating work.
- For compatibility aliases and durable data keys that must remain (`ddx agent execute-loop`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `execute-loop-*` Extra keys, historical event kinds), add concise comments documenting them as legacy/back-compat names and point readers at `ddx work` / `ddx try`.
- Re-scope or update related open beads whose titles/descriptions still establish new `ExecuteLoop*` names (notably ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f) so future work does not add fresh legacy terminology.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not remove the legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` or `ddx agent execute-bead` command aliases in this bead; they are compatibility surfaces unless a governing doc explicitly authorizes removal.
- Do not rename durable tracker keys or historical event kinds if doing so would break existing `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, `.ddx/executions`, worker records, or archive reads. Document retained keys instead.
- Do not change work/try behavior, queue ordering, claim semantics, routing policy, or Fizeau integration.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`; update any related bead metadata only with `ddx bead update` / `ddx bead dep`.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-387a0178: structural C13 file/identifier rename; this bead depends on it or should be folded into it if it is re-authored.
- ddx-29058e2a and ddx-1a9cc01f: ExecuteLoopSpec naming conflicts with the intended WorkSpec/WorkRequest vocabulary; update or supersede as part of this cleanup.
- ddx-eccc6efb: server-spawn migration may delete some `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` paths; if it lands first, shrink this bead to remaining public/API/doc names.",
    "acceptance": "1. Audit report committed at `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/work-try-nomenclature-audit.md` listing every remaining `execute-loop|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead` hit under `cli/`, `docs/`, `.agents/skills/`, and `.claude/skills/`, classified as renamed, retained-compat, durable-key, historical-doc, generated, or follow-up.
2. `TestTerminology_WorkTryPublicSurface` (new or renamed in the appropriate cmd/server test package) verifies public help and server/UI-facing labels prefer `ddx work` / `ddx try`, while legacy `ddx agent execute-loop` / `ddx agent execute-bead` are described only as compatibility aliases.
3. `TestTerminology_DurableLegacyKeysDocumented` verifies retained durable keys such as `execute-loop-retry-after`, `execute-loop-last-status`, and `execute-loop-last-detail` have explicit comments/docs explaining why they remain legacy-named.
4. All non-compatibility production symbols introduced for the FEAT-010 path are renamed from ExecuteLoop/ExecuteBead vocabulary to Work/Try vocabulary, or each exception is listed in the audit report with a compatibility reason.
5. Related open beads ddx-387a0178, ddx-29058e2a, and ddx-1a9cc01f are updated, superseded, or explicitly cross-referenced so no open bead still instructs future work to create new `ExecuteLoop*` names without a compatibility rationale.
6. `rg -n "execute-loop|ExecuteLoop|execute loop|execute-bead|ExecuteBead|execute bead" cli docs .agents/skills .claude/skills` returns only audit-approved retained compatibility/durable/historical hits documented in `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/work-try-nomenclature-audit.md`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestTerminology|TestWork|TestTry|TestExecuteLoop|TestExecuteBead" -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/...` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.",

ABSORBS
- ddx-09d2990c (WIRE) — the 4 specific unwired functions become a subset of this sweep. Close WIRE as superseded by this bead at file time.
- Any clusters from /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md not already covered.
- Codex audit additions: jsonlFallbackForCollection (cli/internal/bead/backend_jsonl.go:90), Store.breakStaleLock (lock.go:70), NewStoreWithBackend (store.go:119), WithSpokeHTTPClient (federation_spoke.go:103), Server.execStore (server.go:2028), GraphQL NewResolver (resolver.go:14).

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

DEP: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744), REACH-GO (ddx-ea76e1b8)

NOT IN SCOPE
- The protocol or check implementation itself (separate beads).
- Other languages (this project is Go-only at the check layer).
- Auto-run as recurring sweep (could be a future /schedule offer; not built into this bead).",

data.text: I’ve got the parallel-landing chaos bead and the contentions/metrics bead candidates. I’m doing one last lookup for the exact lock-metrics bead title so the doc can cite both IDs cleanly, then I’ll patch in the playbook section.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n 'WithLock wait time|lock wait duration|tracker_lock.go withTrackerLock|agent/lock: instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time|instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time' .ddx/beads.jsonl"

data.output:
29:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/bead/lock.go WithLock records wait-duration and hold-duration via agentmetrics or equivalent observable channel.
2. cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock records wait, hold, and retry-count metrics.
3. When tracker_lock.go exhausts DefaultLockRetryPolicy.MaxElapsed, the resulting error message includes the lock identity and the holder's claimant-PID (not just a generic timeout).
4. TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration in cli/internal/bead/lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts the metric is captured for a contended lock.
5. TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount in cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock_test.go (or new test file) asserts retry count metric is captured under simulated contention.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... green.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4386b853238b444647603a77e58da0e744369010","created_at":"2026-05-09T20:58:19.197933907Z","description":"PROBLEM
  When parallel-worker drain mode is enabled (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently), bead.Store.WithLock and the agent-layer
  tracker locks become a contention surface. Today there is no
  observability into lock wait time, lock-hold duration, or contention
  rate — failures manifest as 30s MaxElapsed exhaustion and
  resource_exhausted outcomes, with no telemetry to debug.

  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified this as a
  prerequisite for safely scaling up to N=2..4 parallel workers: without
  per-lock metrics, we cannot tell whether a 10s WithLock wait
  (lock.go:18-27 default) is approaching its budget under load, or
  whether 30s tracker-lock MaxElapsed (tracker_lock.go:51) is being
  exhausted by a long-tail of concurrent acquisitions vs occasional
  hot-path blocking.

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27 WithLock has no instrumentation; it
    spins at 50ms intervals and waits up to 10s by default. Wait time is
    not measured or emitted.
  - cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183 withTrackerLock /
    withMainGitLock acquire/release path has no instrumentation.
  - There is no operator-facing dashboard, log, or metric for lock
    contention.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add lightweight metrics around three lock surfaces:

  1. bead.Store.WithLock (cli/internal/bead/lock.go:18-27):
     - Record lock-wait duration (time from WithLock entry to lock
       acquisition).
     - Record lock-hold duration (time the callback held the lock).
     - Emit via the existing agentmetrics package (or a new bead-metrics
       channel) keyed by call site.

  2. withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock (cli/internal/bead/tracker_lock.go:105-183):
     - Same wait/hold instrumentation.
     - Additional: record retry-attempt count when DefaultLockRetryPolicy
       is consumed (so we see "needed 3 retries" vs "got it on first
       attempt").

  3. Exit code / stop reason aggregation:
     - When a worker times out at MaxElapsed, capture the lock identity
       and the holder's claimant-PID at point of exhaustion. Today the
       error message is generic.

  Surface format:
     - Existing agentmetrics output (likely .ddx/agent-metrics or
       similar; check cli/internal/agentmetrics for existing API).
     - Or stdout JSON line per lock acquire/release if no agentmetrics
       sink exists.

  Add tests:
     - TestWithLock_RecordsWaitDuration: assert metric is captured for a
       contended lock.
     - TestTrackerLock_RecordsRetryCount: assert retry count metric is
       captured under simulated contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Fixing lock contention itself (sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes the
    big contributor; this bead is observability for what's left).
  - Building a lock-contention dashboard (separate concern; just emit
    the data).
  - Reducing the 10s/30s budgets (those are correct; the metric tells
    us if they're tight).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (network-free Land()) drops the big
    contention cause; this bead provides visibility into what remains.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a (chaos for stale-lock recovery) covers
    correctness; this bead covers visibility.
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain
    landing under shared-main contention" needs this metrics surface to
    write meaningful assertions.","events_attachment":"ddx-128c7f9d/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-128c7f9d","issue_type":"chore","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:feature","observability","prevention"],"priority":0,"queue-rank":"7","session_id":"eb-2b4c3d8d","status":"closed","title":"agent/lock: instrument bead.Store WithLock wait time + tracker-lock acquisition metrics","updated_at":"2026-05-10T15:53:25.567407957Z"}

2. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve asserting exactly one of three conflicting lands succeeds; the other two preserve correctly.
3. The same file contains TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded with N=4 workers over M=10 cycles, asserting preservation rate < 50%.
4. Tests use deterministic timing (channels or synctest) and per-test sandbox via testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir().
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestConcurrentLand_' green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-12T02:35:01Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"4014696","closing_commit_sha":"79f9dcd7b2ae05be4b6cd0d7a750b4021a1cd4f1","created_at":"2026-05-09T20:58:19.496585499Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-42741260","depends_on_id":"ddx-1f774edf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-09T20:58:37Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
  After sister bead ddx-1f774edf removes network I/O from Land(), the
  drain becomes safe for external parallelism (multiple ddx work --once
  shells running concurrently). However, parallel workers will land their
  own bead's worktree against the same local main and may produce
  overlapping changes — multi-worker conflict frequency on Land()'s
  git merge step is unmeasured. The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus
  self-review identified this as a key risk: under high parallelism,
  preservation (resource_exhausted to iteration ref) could become the
  common path rather than the exception, defeating the gain.

  This bead adds chaos coverage for the parallel-landing case and a
  sentinel that fails if the preservation rate exceeds an acceptable
  threshold under simulated load.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go and chaos2_test.go cover
    bead.Store-layer concurrency only.
  - cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_test.go covers single-worker
    semantics.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a covers stale-lock recovery for a single
    worker's local-only land. It does not cover N>1 workers landing in
    parallel.
  - No test verifies what happens when worker A lands branch-A into
    main, then worker B (whose worktree was branched from pre-A main)
    tries to land branch-B with overlapping changes.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add three chaos tests in cli/internal/agent/landing_chaos_test.go (new
  file, or extend tracker_lock_test.go):

  1. TestConcurrentLand_NonOverlappingChanges_BothLandClean:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each with its own bead/worktree
       touching disjoint files.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: all three land on local main, in some order; no
       preservation; main contains all three commits.

  2. TestConcurrentLand_OverlappingChanges_OneLandsRestPreserve:
     - Spawn 3 worker goroutines, each touching the same file with
       different content.
     - All three call Land() concurrently.
     - Assert: exactly one lands; the other two are preserved to
       iteration refs; the preserved branches are intact and contain
       their bead's full work.

  3. TestConcurrentLand_PreservationRateUnderLoadIsBounded:
     - Spawn N worker goroutines (N=4) over M cycles (M=10), each with
       a randomized mix of overlapping/non-overlapping changes.
     - Assert: preservation rate is < 50% (i.e., at least half of
       attempts land cleanly). This is a smoke check that parallel
       drain is viable, not a hard SLA.

  Use deterministic timing (channels or synctest), not wall-clock.
  Use testutils.NewFixtureRepo or t.TempDir() per test.
  Depends on sister bead ddx-1f774edf so the lock holds are sub-second
  (otherwise the test exercises today's contention bug, not the
  parallel-landing concern).

NON-SCOPE
  - Multi-process chaos (these are goroutine-level concurrent landings
    using fake LandingGitOps that mirror the real merge logic).
  - Coordinating workers via a "land queue" abstraction (separate
    architectural concern; this bead establishes the baseline measurement
    against which any such work would be evaluated).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister bead ddx-1f774edf (depends on it landing first).
  - Sister bead (just-filed) "agent/lock: instrument bead.Store
    WithLock wait time" — its metrics are referenced by these tests'
    assertions if available.
  - Sister bead ddx-c019e48a complements this for the single-worker
    stale-lock case.","events_attachment":"ddx-42741260/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-12T02:35:01.729089895Z","id":"ddx-42741260","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:6","area:agent","area:lock","kind:test","prevention"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"queue-rank":"8","session_id":"eb-f18b8501","status":"closed","title":"agent/chaos: cover concurrent-drain landing under shared-main contention","updated_at":"2026-05-12T02:45:00.616545921Z"}

1	# Parallel drain operator playbook
     2	
     3	Use this playbook only after `ddx-1f774edf` has landed. Before that, `Land()`
     4	still spans network I/O under `withMainGitLock`, so running multiple
     5	`ddx work --once` shells in parallel is unsafe and mostly just creates lock
     6	contention.
     7	
     8	## 1. When To Use Parallel Drain
     9	
    10	Parallel drain is for the post-`ddx-1f774edf` state, when the drain path is
    11	local-only and the bottleneck moves from network round-trips to normal merge
    12	conflicts. If that bead has not landed yet, do not try to scale by opening
    13	more shells.
    14	
    15	## 2. How To Start
    16	
    17	Open `N` tmux panes or terminal shells and run a separate `ddx work --once`
    18	loop in each one. The goal is external parallelism, not a new `ddx work
    19	--parallel N` flag.
    20	
    21	## 3. Mix Harnesses
    22	
    23	Do not run all workers on the same harness if you want true throughput.
    24	Mixing harnesses avoids serializing on a single account or provider quota.
    25	
    26	Example:
    27	
    28	```sh
    29	# Pane 1
    30	ddx work --once --harness claude --max-cost 25
    31	
    32	# Pane 2
    33	ddx work --once --harness codex --max-cost 25
    34	
    35	# Pane 3
    36	ddx work --once --harness openrouter --max-cost 25
    37	```
    38	
    39	If `openrouter` is not the right local name in your environment, use an
    40	equivalent third harness or provider-backed shell. The point is to spread load
    41	across different rate-limit buckets instead of pinning every worker to the same
    42	one.
    43	
    44	## 4. Budget The Cost Ceiling
    45	
    46	Each `ddx work` invocation accepts `--max-cost`, and that ceiling applies per
    47	shell. Budget the session as the sum across all `N` shells, not as one shared
    48	global cap.
    49	
    50	## 5. Expect Some Preservation
    51	
    52	Parallel landing does not mean every attempt lands cleanly. Under load, some
    53	workers will preserve their iteration ref instead of merging immediately. That
    54	is normal. Keep the preserved refs and merge them manually once the shared-main
    55	conflict clears.
    56	
    57	See `ddx-42741260` for chaos coverage of that preservation behavior under load.
    58	
    59	## 6. Watch The Metrics
    60	
    61	When contention looks suspicious, check the lock-wait, lock-hold, and retry
    62	metrics emitted by `ddx-128c7f9d`. Those are the operator-visible channels for
    63	debugging whether the remaining bottleneck is lock acquisition, merge pressure,
    64	or something else.
    65	
    66	## Notes
    67	
    68	- This playbook is about external shells. It does not introduce a new router or
    69	  worker-spawn design.
    70	- The single-worker harness selection guidance still applies inside each shell;
    71	  this section only adds the parallel-drain operator pattern.
